<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966174 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06058: Removing unresolved reference

- page_id: `243966174`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966174/Removing+unresolved+reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:54.885+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305661011

305661013

305661012

At times, a reference to some recovered element is no longer needed and can be removed.

To remove a reference to the recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

The reference to the particular recovered element is removed.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the referring elements are non-editable, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

You can remove all unresolved references (including all used modules) of a recovered element in one operation.

To remove all references to a recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

All unresolved references to the particular recovered element are removed. As the unresolved references are cleared, the recovered element is removed as well.

Unresolved references will not be removed from the referring elements, if they are stored in a read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked.

305661010

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replacing recovered element with another element' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='']

## PAGE 06062: Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `249565499`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565499/Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:35.467+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| CapabilityProperty | CapabilityRole |
| SecurityProperty* | InformationRole |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Concern | systemConcern | enterprisePhase |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed:**

- Condition::conditionKind ta g was removed. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping solutions and issues' space=''] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968529 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06063: Reordering elements

- page_id: `243968529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968529/Reordering+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:01.699+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:

To reorder elements in dialogs

1. Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Up (to move up) or Down (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog
  - Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard [ATTACHMENT filename='customize diagram wizard dialog.png']
  - Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:</p><p>To reorder elements in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the <strong>Up </strong>(to move up) or<strong> Down</strong> (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog</li><li>Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize diagram wizard dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_and_dropping_element_in_a_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29652724-5bc7-4acd-add7-62624c32125c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565563 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06064: Replaced and renamed elements

- page_id: `249565563`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565563/Replaced+and+renamed+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:01:22.750+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration > Elements mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency] | Maps To Capability [Abstraction] | Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability. |
| Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency] | Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction] | Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform. |
| Actual Organization Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility. |
| Actual Property | Actual Measurement | Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement. |
| Capability Of Performer [Dependency] | Exhibits [Abstraction] | Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions. |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |  |
| ClimateLight Condition | Environment | A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind. |
| Deployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneOut Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneActual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:In Service.Deployed.No Longer Used.Out Of Service.Other. |
| EnergyMateriel | Natural Resource | Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal. |
| Exchange Element | Data ElementInformation Element | If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform). |
| Functional Standard | Standard | A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Functional Control Flow [Control Flow]Functional Object Flow [Object Flow] | Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly. |
| Project | Actual Project | Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs. |
| Individual Person Role | Actual Post | An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America" where the Post would be president. |
| Location | Actual Location | Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc. |
| Location Type | Location | Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located. |
| Logical Architecture | Operational Architecture | Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective. |
| Measure | Actual Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements. |
| Measure TypeSecurity Attributes Group | Measurement Set | Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements. |
| Milestone Sequence | Milestone Dependency | Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another. |
| Needline | Operational Connector | Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges. |
| Node Operation | Operational Method | Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity. |
| Node Parent | Operational Agent | An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer. |
| Node Port | Operational Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created. |
| Node Role | Operational Role | Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]Operational Object Flow [Object Flow] | Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly. |
| Organizational Project RelationshipProject Ownership | Responsible For | Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For. |
| Organization Type | Organization | Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose. |
| NodePerformerSecurity Domain | Operational Performer | Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources. |
| Person Type | Post | Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer). |
| Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective. |
| Project Type | Project | Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs. |
| Property | Measurement | Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. |
| RequestService | Operational PortResource Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port. |
| Resource Interaction | Resource Exchange |  |
| Resource Interaction Item | Resource Exchange Item | Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange. |
| Resource Interface | Resource Connector | Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles. |
| Resource State Machine | Resource State Description | A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions. |
| Resource Operation | Resource Method |  |
| Role Type | Responsibility | Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Service Access | Capability ConfigurationService Specification | Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Attribute | Flow Property | Flow Property is a SysML v1 element with already defined direction that is inout. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports. |
| Service Channel | Resource ConnectorOperational Connector | If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector. |
| Service Level Value Set | Provided Service Level | Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider. |
| Service State Machine | Service State Description | Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions. |
| Skill | Competence | Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude. |
| Skill Of Person Type | Provides Competence | Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies. |
| System | Resource Artifact | Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.). |
| System Resource | Resource Performer | The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource |
| Service Operation | Service Method | A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function. |
| Technical Standard | Standard | Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Trustline | Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Vision | Enterprise Vision | An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved. |
| Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]Overlap [Dependency] | Dependency | The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap. |
| Association Of Information [Association]Entity Relationship [Association] | Association | The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship. |
| Asynchronous Message [Signal] | Signal | The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message. |
| Condition Property [Property]Entity Attribute [Property] | Property | The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute. |
| Design Rule [Constraint] | Constraint | The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule. |
| Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification] | Instance Specification | The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent . |
| Logical Data Model [Data Model]Physical Data Model [Data Model] | Data Model | The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model. |
| Operational Event Trace [Interaction]Resource Event Trace [Interaction]Service Interaction [Interaction] | Interaction | The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction. |
| Operational State [State]Resource State [State] | State | The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State. |
| Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Activity Edge | The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. |
| Service Description [Package] | Package | The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description. |
| Service Level Value [Slot] | Slot | The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value. |
| Service Message Handler [Reception] | Reception | The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler. |
| UPDM Element | UAF Element |  |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element | Property UPDM 2.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |

relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Asynchronous Message [Signal]</td><td colspan="1">Signal</td><td colspan="1">The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Condition Property [Property]</p><p>Entity Attribute [Property]</p></td><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Design Rule [Constraint]</td><td colspan="1">Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification]</td><td colspan="1">Instance Specification</td><td colspan="1">The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent .</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Logical Data Model [Data Model]</p><p>Physical Data Model [Data Model]</p></td><td colspan="1">Data Model</td><td colspan="1">The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Resource Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Service Interaction [Interaction]</p></td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td><td colspan="1">The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational State [State]</p><p>Resource State [State]</p></td><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1">The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1">Activity Edge</td><td colspan="1">The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Description [Package]</td><td colspan="1">Package</td><td colspan="1">The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value [Slot]</td><td colspan="1">Slot</td><td colspan="1">The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Message Handler [Reception]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Reception</p></td><td colspan="1">The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element</td><td colspan="1">UAF Element</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following table describes properties  which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Property UPDM 2.1 [name]</p></th><th><p>Property UAF 1.1 [name]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Viewpoint</td><td colspan="1"><p>languages</p><p>methods </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>language</p><p>method</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968236 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06065: Replacing elements

- page_id: `243968236`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968236/Replacing+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:54.055+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

###### Original element

The original element is the element that is going to be replaced. In other words, the replacement source.

###### Replacement target

The replacement target is the element with which the original element is going to be replaced.

###### Replaced element

The replaced element is the element to which the original element has been changed during the replacement.

###### Element properties

The element properties are so called the element specification properties (specified in the element Specification window) and the element symbol properties (defined in the element Symbol Properties dialog).

###### Inner elements

Inner elements are the elements stored inside the element (that is, owned by that element), for example, the class as inner package element.

You can replace one model element with another of the same type (metaclass) element. The model element replacement is useful when during the modeling process you notice that one model element needs to be replaced with another. All relations and references of the original element are transferred to the newly replaced element.

After the replacement, an original element will be replaced with a replacement target:

- The replacement target will be displayed in all diagrams instead of the original element.
- All references to the original element will be replaced by references to the replacement target.
- The replacement target after the replacement will have all the relationships of the replacement target and the original element.
- The original element will be deleted from the project.
- The original element properties and inner elements will be deleted from the project.

See the replacement example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The preceding figure illustrates the replacement of the *User* class instead of the *Customer* class. After the replacement the *User* class has been removed and the *Customer* class has been created instead of it.

INLINE

To replace one element with another

1. Select a symbol on the diagram pane or an element in the Model Browser and open its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Replace With . The warning message appears:
  - Click Yes to continue the element replacement. The properties and inner elements of the original element will be lost.
  - Click No to cancel the element replacement. The selected element will not be replaced.
3. If you decided to continue the replacement, the element selection dialog opens.
4. In the element selection dialog, select the element with which you want to replace the original element.
5. Click **OK**. The original element is replaced with the target element.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966173 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06066: Replacing recovered element with another element

- page_id: `243966173`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966173/Replacing+recovered+element+with+another+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:54.829+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305622476

305622479

305622477

At times, you may decide to reference a different element rather than the recovered one in the model.

To replace a reference from a recovered element to another one

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Use Another Element .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Use Another Element .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . The reference is now pointing to the selected element. Also, it is no longer unresolved.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the unresolved references are read-only, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

Alternatively, you can replace all references from a particular recovered element to another one using one operation.

To replace a recovered element with another one in all references

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Validation > Recovered Element > Change Usages To .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Change Usages To .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . All the references are now pointing to another element. Also, they are no longer decorated as unresolved.

305622474

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing unresolved reference' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970587 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06068: Report Data Management Pane

- page_id: `243970587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970587/Report+Data+Management+Pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:51.421+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1277078685

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1277078691

1277078679

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1277078690

Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. The built-in report data template consists of several report data variables such as title, author, scope, and purpose that you can modify and adjust to meet your needs. You can also create a new report data template as needed, and manage the variables using the report data management pane.

This section defines report data, and how to use it in your report to represent variables that you want to include. Click the following links to navigate to the content of this page.

- 
- 
- 
- 

Reportdata

#### Report Data and Variables

A report in Report Wizard has a collection of variables which are called report data. Report Wizard provides a built-in report data for every predefined template. The built-in report data comes with predefined variables and values which you can edit or delete. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.

You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. Report Wizard also allows you to create a report data as an element in the Containment tree inside a project by using the Report Profile which allows you to commit the Report Data to Teamwork Cloud, and share it with other users.

Reportdatapane

#### Report Data Management Pane

Report Wizard stores the built-in report data in the **Report Data**management pane which helps you to organize information into groups, and keep revision history created in a project.

This pane also allows you to:

- Create, edit, delete, or clone a Report Data.
- Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the built-in report data in the Report Data management pane.

The following table lists the buttons and their function in the **Report Data**management pane.

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Creates report data. |
|  | Edits report data. |
|  | Deletes report data. |
|  | Clones report data. |
|  | Creates a report variable. |

WorkingwithReportdata

#### Working with Report Data

You can create report data either in:

- Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.
- A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.

The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.

To create report data in Report Wizard

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **New****Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.
4. Enter a new report name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Before creating report data in a project, you need to use a report profile,*Report Profile.mdzip*, which is located in the*<install.root>\profiles*folder.

To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)

1. On the main menu, click **File******> **Use Project** > **Use Local Project**. The **Use Project** dialog will open, and the project module path *<install.root>\profiles* will be selected by default. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Selecting a project module path for thereport profile and Report Profile.mdzip in the Use Project dialog.
2. Select *Report Profile*and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Specify the usage options. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Usage Options.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.

create new report data in MD project

To create a Report Data in a project

1. Use *Report Profile.mdzip* in your project.
2. Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select **Create Element**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating Report Data in the project
3. Select **Report Data** from the menu.
4. Type the name of the Report Data element in the Containment tree.

You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.

| Tag Values | Function |
| --- | --- |
| autoImageSize | Change the image size. |
| imageFormat | Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG. |
| emptyText | Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable. |
| data | Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard. |
| template | Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data. |
| generateRecursively | Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively. |

To edit report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The**Edit Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog
4. Edit the report’s name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To delete report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Confirm delete** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Confirm delete dialog.
4. Click either [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] to delete the selected data or [ATTACHMENT filename='No_button.png'] to cancel the operation.

To clone report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Clone Report** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.
4. Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with **Copy of** (cloned report name).
5. Click[IMAGE alt='' src='']. The cloned Report Data will then be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.

When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.

WorkingwithReportvariables

#### Working with Report Data Variables

Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the **Specification** dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write **$Author** in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.

Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.

To create a report variable in a project

1. Right-click a **Report Data** in the Containment tree and select **Create Element** > **Variable**. The variable will appear inside the **Report Data**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.

You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the **Report Variable** dialog in Report Wizard.

To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='New_button.png'] . The New Variable dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the Owner box.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.

To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select an empty value in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.

To create a child variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select**Parent** in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.

3. The new variable will appear in the table in the **Report Variable** dialog.

4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the variables in the Report Data.

To edit a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] . The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.

To delete a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click a variable in the table, and then click **Delete**. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] and the variable will be deleted.

To get the value of a top-level variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type: **$Parent**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to a parent variable in the template.
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose Rich Text Format (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New** **Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] >**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. The**Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following **My Report Data** as an example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### My Report Data as an example.

To get the value of the child of a variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type any of the following to print a child variable. (i) **$Parent.get(1)**to get a child value by index. (ii) **$Parent.get(“Child”)** to get a child value by name comparison. (iii) **$Parent.Child**to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to the child variable in the template
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose **Rich Text Format** (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The resulting RTF file.

#### Glossary

- Clone To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.

- RTF Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.

#### More information about working with reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the command line' space='']

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']250

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4db7214-d3dc-499d-90ea-77da7bd57240"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078685</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f93fb5-fb8b-43cd-91b4-80a0bf5b2a74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078691</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a25830c1-cfcf-4066-856c-083cc4ce0976"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d916590-ea04-46c4-86b6-fee4fa116189"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21d8533e-1f56-4f37-99f9-489fa8427806"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078690</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. The built-in report data template consists of several report data variables such as title, author, scope, and purpose that you can modify and adjust to meet your needs. You can also create a new report data template as needed, and manage the variables using the report data management pane.</p><p>This section defines report data, and how to use it in your report to represent variables that you want to include. Click the following links to navigate to the content of this page.</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data and Variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reportdatapane"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data Management pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Report Data]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportvariables"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Report Data Variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54b10626-197a-4056-b21d-7620bb95addb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reportdata</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Report Data and Variables</span></h2><p>A report in Report Wizard has a collection of variables which are called report data. Report Wizard provides a built-in report data for every predefined template. The built-in report data comes with predefined variables and values which you can edit or delete. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BuiltinVariables.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.</h6><p>You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. Report Wizard also allows you to create a report data as an element in the Containment tree inside a project by using the Report Profile which allows you to commit the Report Data to Teamwork Cloud, and share it with other users.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eac4b8e3-d7df-4514-80f6-25bf3295f39a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reportdatapane</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Report Data Management Pane</h2><p>Report Wizard stores the built-in report data in the <strong>Report Data </strong>management pane which helps you to organize information into groups, and keep revision history created in a project. </p><p>This pane also allows you to:</p><ul><li>Create, edit, delete, or clone a Report Data.</li><li>Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Built-in Report Data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting the built-in report data in the Report Data management pane.</h6><p>The following table lists the buttons and their function in the <strong>Report Data </strong>management pane.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td>Creates report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png" /></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Edits report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png" /></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Deletes report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png" /></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Clones report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png" /></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Creates a report variable.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18d004f5-45e4-4a94-9453-e0b0858b4c4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithReportdata</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Working with Report Data</h2><p>You can create report data either in:</p><ul><li>Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.</li><li>A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.</p><p>To create report data in Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>New </strong><strong>Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="New Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-report-data-a.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.</h6></li><li>Enter a new report name and description, and then click<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>.</li></ol><p>Before <span>creating report data in a project, you <span>need to use a report profile, </span><em>Report Profile.mdzip</em><span>, which is located in the </span><em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em><span> folder. </span></span></p><p>To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong><strong> </strong>&gt; <strong>Use Project</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Local Project</strong>. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog will open, and the project module path <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em> will be selected by default.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseLocalProject_menu.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project.</h6><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_select.png" /></ac:image><br />Selecting a project module path for the<span> report profile and Report Profile.mdzip</span> in the Use Project dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Select <em>Report Profile </em>and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Specify the usage options.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UsageOptions_select.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Usage Options.</h6></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Finish_button.png" /></ac:image>. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.</p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="140d6f37-2e15-4467-9f5d-5bed364565bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create new report data in MD project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To create a Report Data in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Use <em>Report Profile.mdzip</em> in your project.</p></li><li><p>Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_ReportData.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating Report Data in the project</span></h6></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Data</strong> from the menu.</p></li><li>Type the name of the <strong>Report Data</strong> element in the Containment tree.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03eb30b1-3dc1-478d-b1fe-919fdfbbc25f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Tag Values</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>autoImageSize</td><td>Change the image size.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>imageFormat</td><td>Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>emptyText</td><td>Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>data</td><td><p>Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard.</p></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">template</td><td colspan="1">Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">generateRecursively</td><td colspan="1">Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png" /></ac:image>. The<strong> Edit Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Edit Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="Edit Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-report-data-a.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog</h6></li><li><p>Edit the report’s name and description, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target">To delete report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Confirm delete</strong> dialog will appear.    </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Confirm Delete Dialog" ac:alt="Confirm Delete Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm-delete-report-data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The Confirm delete dialog.</h6></li><li>Click either <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png" /></ac:image> to delete the selected data or <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="No_button.png" /></ac:image> to cancel the operation.</li></ol><p>To clone report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Clone Report</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Clone Report Dialog" ac:alt="Clone Report Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone-report-b.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with <strong>Copy of</strong> (cloned report name).</p></li><li><p><span>Click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image><span>. The cloned Report Data will then be created.</span></p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Copy of My Report.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ccbe5a1-e65d-4424-8267-cab5e66c46a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38ad4cab-6003-4d7d-9f6e-d9583c22eb71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithReportvariables</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Working with Report Data Variables</h2><p>Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write <strong>$Author</strong> in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.</p><p>Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.</p><p>To create a report variable in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Right-click a <strong>Report Data</strong> in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Variable</strong>. The variable will appear inside the <strong>Report Data</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_Variable.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.</span></h6></li></ol><p>You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog in Report Wizard.</p><p>To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Variable</strong> dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the <strong>Owner</strong> box.</li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="New variable dialog" ac:alt="New variable dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-b.png" /></ac:image><br />Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select an empty value in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Parent Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Parent Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-c.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a child variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select<strong> Parent</strong> in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Child Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Child Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-d.png" /></ac:image><br />Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.</p><p>     3.  The new variable will appear in the table in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p><p>     4.  Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> to save the variables in the Report Data.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="browse_button.png" /></ac:image>. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Editing Variables" ac:alt="Editing Variables"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-e.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>. The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.</li></ol><p>To delete a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Click a variable in the table, and then click <strong>Delete</strong>. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Deleting Variable" ac:alt="Deleting Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete-variable-b.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png" /></ac:image> and the variable will be deleted.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of a top-level variable<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type: <strong>$Parent</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to a parent variable in the template.</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose Rich Text Format (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png" /></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image> &gt;<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>. The<strong> Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png" /></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parent_result.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b50bd350-075c-47d5-b0ac-c193e56e5d64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following <strong>My Report Data</strong> as an example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Report Data" ac:alt="Sample of Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sample of report data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">My Report Data as an example.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of the child of a variable</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type any of the following to print a child variable.</p><p>(i) <strong>$Parent.get(1) </strong>to get a child value by index.</p><p>(ii) <strong>$Parent.get(“Child”) </strong> to get a child value by name comparison.</p><p>(iii) <strong>$Parent.Child </strong>to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to the child variable in the template</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose <strong>Rich Text Format</strong> (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png" /></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png" /></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="child_result.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><br /></span></p></li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span>The resulting RTF file.</span></h6><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Clone</strong><br />To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.</li></ul><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>RTF</strong><br />Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with reports in Report Wizard</h2><p class="confluence-link">To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Containment Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating reports from the command line" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Command Line]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Sample model</h2><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="view-file" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f35fc673-0b17-4051-9a7d-cf664c12b0b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">250</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970668 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06069: Report Designer

- page_id: `243970668`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970668/Report+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:52.724+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1769283899

1769283902

1769283901

Report Designer is a tool for conveniently drafting your template in Microsoft Word Document (DOCX) and Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet (XLSX) formats. Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.

The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.

982760273

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.</p><p>The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c909faa3-1543-4f51-9ae0-4ac92c125ded"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">982760273</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e88b85d-c02a-425c-a379-205e7a4d36b5" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

$report is a utility tool enabling a template to get the modeling tool data.

##### **$report.createValueSpecificationText(specification)**

Create texts representing the ValueSpecification element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | specification | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.ValueSpecification | A given Value Specification. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A String value for ValueSpecification. |

****

##### **$report.filterDiagram(diagramList, diagramTypes)**

Return a collection of diagrams from a list of diagrams filtered by types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | diagramList | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagrams. |
| diagramTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered diagrams. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElement(elementList, humanTypes)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by human types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| humanTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of human types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can change the human type depending on the language used in the modeling tool. For example, "Class" is the human type of the classes in the English modeling tool while "Classe" is the human type for the classes in French. The specified humanTypes needs to be changed when you change the language used in the modeling tool.

For example:

```text

```

****

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| elementTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of element types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of property names. |

For example:

```text
$pp.value
#end]]>
```

By default, all properties are exported. They can be filtered by adding a "STANDARD" or "EXPERT" argument, e.g.*$report.getElementProperties($element, "STANDARD").*

Return a comment attached to an element. If no comment is attached, it will return a null value.

|  | Name | Type | Description |

| Return | - | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Comment | A comment instance of the element. |

****

##### **$report.getElementName(element)**

Get an element’s name. If the name is empty, it will return "<unnamed>". This method performs the following procedures:

- If the element is generalized from the NamedElement metaclass, it will return $element.name
- If the element is generalized from the Slot metaclass, it will return $element.definingFeature.name
- If the element is a UML element, it will return $element.humanName
- Else returns $element.toString()

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | java.lang.Object | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An element name. |

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(element)**

Return an image icon of an element with a default icon filename (“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of the element's icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

##### $report.getImageFor(element, dpi)

Return an image with specified DPI of an element.

| Name | Type | Description | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| dpi | int | DPI of the element image. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object with specified DPI. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The DPI value can be 1 to 4800.
- If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI set in *Template* and *Global* will be used respectively. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space=''] .
- If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800 .

##### **$report.getIconFor(element, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon of an element. The icon name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon’s name is set as***”icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode***value equals false, the icon name is set as

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling tool element. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix of an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix of an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code of an icon as the icon name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type)**

Return an image icon for an element type with a default icon’s filename ((“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon for an element type. The icon’s name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon name is set as ***“icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode*** value equals false, the icon name is set as ***prefix + element type + suffix***.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix for an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix for an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code ofan icon as the icon’s name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | diagram | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram | A target diagram. |
| element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | A presentation element on a given diagram. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.List | Returns the polygonal bounds of an element. If the element is not found in the diagram, it will return null. |

****

Return the polygonal bounds of an element. The bounds specify this component's coordinate to its diagram.

Return the rectangular bounds of an element. The bounds specify the component coordinates to its diagram.

|  | Name | Type | Description |

| element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | A presentation element on a given diagram. |  |

##### **$report.getQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name. A qualified name is the name that allows a ***NamedElement***to be identified within a hierarchy of nested ***Namespaces***. It is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the ***NamedElement***itself.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****

##### **$report.getPackageQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name by considering only **Packages** and a given element. **Models** and **Profiles** will not be included in the qualified name.

For example, given the element hierarchy:

```text
com : Package -> nomagic : Package -> ui -> Package -> BaseDialog : Class]]>
```

```text

```

If $class is a “BaseDialog” element, the result from the above template code will be as follows.

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****
