The constraint expression is then modified to<em> {tm=clm+html+ctm+snm+m}{tm&lt;=20}</em>.</h6><h3 class="_mce_tagged_br">Performing automated Requirements verification in Requirement Table </h3><p>The automated Requirements verification analysis can be done directly in the Requirement Table. The analysis evaluates whether the property's value that satisfies the Requirement falls within a range of upper and lower bounds extracted from the Requirement text. Additionally, the automatically calculated margin value helps to determine how close the system model is to fulfilling Requirements.  </p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To perform automated Requirements verification</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2e3ff7cd-a301-4a03-93e8-8653243c0623"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>a Requirement Table. You can also use an existing Requirement Table. </li><li data-uuid="d7d79893-b95c-4b84-8c53-cf130704ccf4"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the scope for the table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the scope]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the table.</li><li data-uuid="351aa735-46a0-413c-b2c6-00e0fdcded5b"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the context element (i.e., a Block as the table context) to perform the context-specific analysis. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The Requirement Table with the <em>Property</em>, <em>Bounds</em>, <em>Value,</em> and <em>Margin</em> columns is created, and passing/failing Requirements are marked (see an image below). </p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirements_verification_example_1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the Requirement patterns mechanism, the constraint extracted from the Requirement text is shown in the <strong>Bounds</strong> column. The <strong>Value</strong> column shows the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Initial Values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ initial value ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (if it exists) or the default value of the Value Property. Finally, the <strong>Margin</strong> column displays the difference between the calculated and required values.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6c2babd-be53-4fd3-8f38-54f6814b4463"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Verification context in tables</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="6d8b19d9-09c6-40a8-b5e0-2c513795bb9b">If the<strong> </strong>table <strong>context</strong> is unspecified, properties satisfying the Requirements are collected from the entire model.</li><li data-uuid="1fcf50cd-ac7e-4bc6-b166-3deebacebabc">When the table <strong>context</strong> is specified, the Requirement Verification Legend and the verification-specific columns are displayed in the table if they were not before:<br /><ul><li><strong>Property</strong> - the Value Property that satisfies the Requirement.<strong> <br /></strong></li><li><strong>Bounds</strong> - lower and upper bounds of the required value extracted from the Requirement text. </li><li><strong>Value</strong> - the calculated value (default value or<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Initial Values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ initial value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) of the property that satisfies the Requirement. </li><li><strong>Margin</strong> - the difference between the calculated value and the required value. </li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6496cd6-45a0-4a6a-9f9f-688de43b742f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Requirements verification limitations</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Table context specification limits</strong> <br />The table <strong>context</strong> should be specified with a Block that is recursively composed of no more than 10,000 parts. Otherwise, the <strong>Property</strong>, <strong>Value</strong>, and <strong>Margin </strong>columns are displayed as empty, and the verification analysis is not performed. In such a case, error icons are displayed in the column headers (see image below).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="error_icon_requirement_table.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Requirements refined by Constraint Blocks<br /></strong> <span>The Requirements Verification analysis in the Requirement Table considers constraints extracted directly from the Requirement text only, meaning that the analysis cannot be performed for Requirements refined by Constraint Blocks.</span></p><p><strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Simulation configuration option<br /></span> </strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If an Instance is defined as the Requirement Table context, the Requirements verification analysis does not consider the Simulation configuration option </span> <ac:link ac:anchor="Remember Failure Status option"><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Recording Verification status of runtime values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remember Failure Status]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="_mce_tagged_br">Performing automated Requirements verification in Instance Table</h3><p>You can also carry out Requirements verification analysis in an Instance table. While the verification in a Requirement table allows you to perform the analysis for a single instance set as the context, the verification in an Instance table allows you to perform the analysis for multiple instances simultaneously via the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> option. <span>In an Instance table, the columns represent the properties provided by the classifier specified for the table, and the appropriate cells display the property values specified in the instances you choose to display in the table. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To perform automated Requirements verification in Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b7133c9e-e0e9-4c16-8193-8ffa84f48298"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating an empty Instance Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>an Instance Table.</li><li data-uuid="be813673-fdd9-41d9-9ea0-452331c8e58b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the classifier]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the table (i.e., the Block containing the properties satisfying the requirements).</li><li data-uuid="a8b7034c-8a5e-4009-bb40-dab81a32aa8e">Set the scope for the table or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Filling+the+table+with+instances#Fillingthetablewithinstances-add_existing_instances">add individual instances</a> to the table. </li><li data-uuid="64559b91-1824-4635-8250-25f0c80b55b1">Select the properties to be displayed in table <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting columns to display" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="bfcc1d4e-7b76-4b61-bb86-962101b57f52">In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Options </strong>button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png" /></ac:image>and select <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> to enable the verification.<br />The Instance table displays the selected instances and performs the verification, which is visible via the highlighted cells based on their verification status (definition is specified in the Legend).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Performing_automated_Requirements_verification_in_Instance_Table.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Performing automated Requirements verification using simulation</h3><p>With the help of simulation, you can perform automatic Requirements verification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35116cdd-a32c-4c13-b1a8-a1794274a0c6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To perform automatic Requirements verification, you must have the Cameo Simulation Toolkit installed. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Installation+and+licensing">How to install &gt;&gt;</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To perform automatic Requirements verification using simulation</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="23e4d57d-0437-40b3-8326-b82553d74a0f">Right-click the Block, which contains the Value Property.</li><li data-uuid="13f82b85-45e1-44cd-9c76-8f36d7541b60">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="d2c2711d-c99d-403a-9b56-03eb1d8c55ab"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Question</strong> dialog, c<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick </span> <strong>Yes </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to load the validation rules and validate the model before the simulation or </span> <strong>No </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to simulate the model without validating it.<br /></span></p></li><li data-uuid="d84f0e5d-863e-4ffd-88f5-80e94b57d363">In the Simulation window, click <ac:image ac:title="Start" ac:alt="Start"><ri:attachment ri:filename="start_button.png" /></ac:image>or press F8 to start the simulation.<br />The result indicating whether or not the value is satisfied is shown in the Variables pane. In the following figure, you can see when the Requirement is not satisfied (highlighted in red) and satisfied (highlighted in green). You can change the value directly in the Value cell, and the Requirement constraint is checked automatically.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatic_req_verification.png" /></ac:image> <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b64b6b6c-0080-41bc-8b22-9760f56ebfc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional features of Cameo Simulation Toolkit</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Elements simulation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about how to perform verification for a single element >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Validation and verification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about how to validate the model against a set of validation rules before executing it >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Reviewing Exported Runtime Values in Tables</span></h3><p><span>When you perform Requirements verification using simulation, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export the simulated runtime values to Instance Specifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The verification data (the constraint, requirement, timestamp, margin, and status) is stored within tagged values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype, which is applied to the slot values of the exported Instance Specifications.<br /></span></p><p><span> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="verification_status_tagged_values.png" /></ac:image> <br /></span></p><p>The table below lists the tagged values of the <span>«VerificationStatus»</span> stereotype.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Tag</p></th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>constraint</strong></td><td colspan="1">The name of the constraint against which a value is tested.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>margin</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>The result of comparing the tested value (for a property that satisfies a requirement) with the requirement boundary.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>requirement <br /></strong></p></td><td colspan="1">The name of the requirement against which a value is tested.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>status</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">The verification result (pass or fail).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>timestamp</strong></td><td colspan="1">The timestamp of the first recorded failure.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>Once the instances are enriched with verification-specific data (exported runtime values), you can use them in either <strong>Instance</strong> or <strong>Requirement</strong> tables to review the verification results. But before that, you need to ensure that the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification </strong>option is turned off. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af7ffaf7-c23d-4e4c-bfa8-ca55377afa06"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> option is enabled in Requirement tables and disabled in Instance tables. Make sure to enable/disable it based on the type of verification you want to display.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To learn more, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enable Patterns-Based Verification option" /></ac:link> page.</span></p><h3><span>Webinar: Automated Requirements Verification</span></h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2231d6a-c14d-43cb-b738-e0e289dad026"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eVzKcnafHpE" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971129 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06100: Reset to defaults option

- page_id: `243971129`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971129/Reset+to+defaults+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:05.630+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Reset to Defaults button.

- The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global config.xml , and (iii) template config.xml .
- The template config.xml configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global config.xml configuration settings.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Reset to Defaults Button" ac:alt="Reset to Defaults Button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportOption9.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The Reset to Defaults button.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c6b50cc-101c-4966-a939-4cc55e249b80"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global <em>config.xml</em>, and (iii) template <em>config.xml</em>.</li><li>The template<em> config.xml</em> configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global <em>config.xml</em> configuration settings.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970831 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06101: Resizing fixed-measurement images

- page_id: `243970831`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970831/Resizing+fixed-measurement+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.879+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050277646

2050277651

2050277644

**On this page**

43

2050277650

There are six methods to specify the resulting dimensions in terms of measurements to resize an image, for example, in inch (in), centimeter (cm), millimeter (mm), point (pt), or pixel (px)], and specifying the dot-per-inch (*DPI*) value. Every measurement parameter must be specified in a pair of quote marks.

###### **$image.setSize(image, measureWidth, measureHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in terms of measurements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Parameter(s) | measureWidth | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | measureHeight | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height in terms of measurement while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width in a term of measurement, while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setDPI(dotsPerInches)**

This method is used to set the resolution (dpi) of images. The default value is 96 dpi. The dpi value will only affect the methods that take inches, centimeters, and millimeters as their parameters.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | dotsPerInches | java.lang.Integer | Image resolution (dpi). |
| Return | - | - | - |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘1.5in’, ‘2in’)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 1.5 inches and height to 2 inches. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’)** or **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setDPI(192)** and then **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘0.9in’, ‘1.1in’)**, for example, to set the resolution (dpi) of the image to 192 dots per inch, and then resize the image width to 0.9 inches and height to 1.1 inches. The resulting image size will become 173x211 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

Instead of using **$image.setDPI(192)**, if you set the resolution (dpi) to 144 using **$image.setDPI(144)**, the resulting image size will become 130x158 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1dc9f2dc-529c-4ba4-ab62-e13e9d007e5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277646</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ae3e954-0fbd-4296-8aa6-133aa7ef0489"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277651</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d66e770a-d557-40f6-a22e-a8fdef742c2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2020b523-34e2-4606-94ef-8f2bf2525626"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277650</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are six methods to specify the resulting dimensions in terms of measurements to resize an image, for example, in inch (in), centimeter (cm), millimeter (mm), point (pt), or pixel (px)], and specifying the dot-per-inch (<em>DPI</em>) value. Every measurement parameter must be specified in a pair of quote marks.</p><h4><strong>$image.setSize(image, measureWidth, measureHeight)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in terms of measurements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>measureWidth </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The width of the image <br />in a term of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureHeight</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The height of the image <br />in a term of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image object <br />for the element icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setHeight(image, measureSize)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height in terms of measurement while maintaining the image aspect ratio.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The height of the <br />image in a term <br />of measurement. </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setHeight(image, measureSize, keepRatio)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image  </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The height of the <br />image in a term <br />of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>keepRatio</td><td>java.lang.Boolean </td><td>A flag to maintain <br />the image aspect ratio. </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setWidth(image, measureSize)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width in a term of measurement, while maintaining the image aspect ratio.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The width of the <br />image in a term of <br />measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setWidth(image, measureSize, keepRatio)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained (depending on the keepRatio parameter).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The width of the <br />image in a term of <br />measurement. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>keepRatio</td><td>java.lang.Boolean  </td><td>A flag to maintain the <br />image aspect ratio.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setDPI(dotsPerInches)</strong></h4><p>This method is used to set the resolution (dpi) of images. The default value is 96 dpi. The dpi value will only affect the methods that take inches, centimeters, and millimeters as their parameters.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>dotsPerInches </td><td>java.lang.Integer </td><td>Image resolution (dpi). </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>- </td><td>- </td><td>- </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Use <strong>$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘1.5in’, ‘2in’)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s width to 1.5 inches and height to 2 inches. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.1.setSize1.5inx2in.png" /></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set size function.</h6><p>Use either <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’)</strong> or <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, true)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.2.setHeight45mm.png" /></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set height function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, false)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.3.setHeight45mmfalse.png" /></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set height function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.setDPI(192)</strong> and then <strong>$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘0.9in’, ‘1.1in’)</strong>, for example, to set the resolution (dpi) of the image to 192 dots per inch, and then resize the image width to 0.9 inches and height to 1.1 inches. The resulting image size will become 173x211 pixels. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="5.1.dpi192.png" /></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set DPI function.</h6><p>Instead of using <strong>$image.setDPI(192)</strong>, if you set the resolution (dpi) to 144 using <strong>$image.setDPI(144)</strong>, the resulting image size will become 130x158 pixels. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="5.2.dpi144.png" /></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set DPI function.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970827 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06102: Resizing fixed-pixel images

- page_id: `243970827`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970827/Resizing+fixed-pixel+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050270131

2050270136

2050270129

**On this page**

43

2050270135

There are five methods to specify the resulting dimensions for an image to be resized in pixel size (the size parameters must be positive numbers or -1 number). If the value is -1, it will resize an image to the document paper dimensions. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -1)
```

As shown by the example, the image will be resized to a paper width while maintaining the aspect ratio. The value -1 applies only to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*. If the value is -2, it will resize an image to document paper bounds if and only if image bounds are larger than paper bounds. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -2)
```

Using the value -2 also maintains the image aspect ratio. However, it can only be applied to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*.

****

###### **$image.setSize(image, sizeWidth, sizeHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | sizeWidth | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | sizeHeight | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, 200, 200)**, for example, to resize the image’s width and height to 200 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150)** or **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

Use **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968495 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06103: Resolving not synchronized elements

- page_id: `243968495`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968495/Resolving+not+synchronized+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:00.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''].

In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the **Synchronization** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</p><p>In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966153 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06104: Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages

- page_id: `243966153`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966153/Resolving+unconfirmed+usages+of+packages
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:34.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For the overview of automated project usages and situations when they can become unconfirmed, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Controlling dependency creation between used projects' space=''].

Unconfirmed project usages are caught by special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated project usages' space=''] and displayed as a warning or error validation result. The severity depends on the exact model composition situation, but the solution methods are the same in both cases.

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Model

#### WARNING: Model Usages

Model Usages

These recovered elements are in turn flagged as errors. Hence single unconfirmed usage can cause a lot of error validation results: one for the unconfirmed usage itself (rule abbreviation - NCAMU) and one for each recovered element (rule abbreviation - REF). In this situation, the unconfirmed usage result should be examined first, because solving it may automatically solve all other results.

There are two ways to resolve an unconfirmed used project usage. The usage can be either confirmed or rejected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways for resolving unconfirmed used project usages.

###### Confirming usages

If the used project usage A → B is good and necessary according to the user policy, it can be confirmed, i.e., the user-defined usage is to be created instead of the current unconfirmed automated used project usage.

You can confirm an automated usage

- By using the specific command
- Manually

To confirm an automated usage by using the specific command

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Confirm and use the used project into <used project_name> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']**Used project** wizard opens. The required used project is already selected in the first step of the wizard.
3. Click Finish. The necessary user-defined used project usage will be created.

To confirm an automated usage manually

#### INFO: Example

Example

In this case, you can better control the usage creation process. So if you need, for example, to use not only a required used project but some other one that brings the required used project as a part of it, be advised to confirm the usage manually.

For example, let’s say there are three unconfirmed usages from the project *RadioSet* to used projects *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances.* Instead of confirming each usage, you can create just one user-defined usage *RadioSet → ElectricComponents*, since the used project *ElectricComponents* brings in *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances* as sub-used projects.

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

However, this is the case when you must be sure that the user-defined usage will be created in the right place.

1. In the main menu, click Options > Used Projects . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**U****sed Projects** dialog opens.
2. Select a used project that is the usage target resource.
3. Click the Used project button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']**Used Project** wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the usual procedure (select a required used project and finish or continue to the second wizard step to provide more configuration options for used project usage and then finish).

****

**Rejecting usages**

If the usage A → B is not good according to the user policy, it must be rejected and removed.

You can reject an automated usage

- By using the specific command (for removing underlying model level references)
- Manually (by removing or redirecti ng underlying model level references to different elements)

To reject an automated usage by using the specific command

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Use this way to reject a model-level usage that, for example, was created inadvertently.

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Remove underlying model references from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). All underlying model-level references (the ones that cause this automated usage) will be removed.

Clear Recovered Element Usages

To remove an automated usage manually

In more complex cases, you may want to address each usage individually. Use dependency analysis means to examine what dependencies are there and review them on a case-by-case basis (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''], and so forth).

**Useful commands for preparing to resolve unconfirmed used project usages**

When you need to explore your project composition in detail before attempting to resolve unconfirmed usages, use the following commands for navigating:

- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']
- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='MCS'] for collaboration projects

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For the overview of automated project usages and situations when they can become unconfirmed, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Controlling dependency creation between used projects" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Unconfirmed project usages are caught by special <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automated project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatic validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and displayed as a warning or error validation result. The severity depends on the exact model composition situation, but the solution methods are the same in both cases.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdb65676-3118-4232-ab6f-8a74c6d5464e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Since problematic project usages are not model elements, they cannot be visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. So when the model contains unconfirmed usages, the root package <em>Model </em>is highlighted as incorrect (regardless of which usage is actually problematic).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2245b057-e895-473e-9238-a082c9bdf592"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When there is an error level unconfirmed usage of some used project, then all the elements of the used project, that are referenced from other model places are shown as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing unresolved references" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Recovered Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">These recovered elements are in turn flagged as errors. Hence single unconfirmed usage can cause a lot of error validation results: one for the unconfirmed usage itself (rule abbreviation - NCAMU) and one for each recovered element (rule abbreviation - REF). In this situation, the unconfirmed usage result should be examined first, because solving it may automatically solve all other results. </span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>There are two ways to resolve an unconfirmed used project usage. The usage can be either confirmed or rejected.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="two_ways_for_resolving_unconfirmed_usages.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Two ways for resolving unconfirmed used project usages.</h6><h4>Confirming usages</h4><p>If the used project usage A → B is good and necessary according to the user policy, it can be confirmed, i.e., the user-defined usage is to be created instead of the current unconfirmed automated used project usage.</p><p>You can confirm an automated usage</p><ul><li data-uuid="5249e92d-f463-4fc6-9a10-5e405340d82b">By using the specific command</li><li data-uuid="3941ce09-f16d-400b-8c23-75cc8dff5bb6">Manually</li></ul><p>To confirm an automated usage by using the specific command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="46adece1-c9de-4ad6-b6df-fc9946d5f8ea">Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.</li><li data-uuid="0c2b7291-d7b8-4cc5-912f-7f629faa043d">Select <strong>Confirm</strong> <strong>and use the used project into &lt;used project_name&gt;</strong> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used project</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. The required used project is already selected in the first step of the wizard.</li><li data-uuid="6d3c0219-2425-4fd5-adba-b07945686749">Click <strong>Finish.</strong> The necessary user-defined used project usage will be created. </li></ol><p>To confirm an automated usage manually</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bdde69e-abc7-48e4-b330-b66f9f20e958"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this case, you can better control the usage creation process. So if you need, for example, to use not only a required used project but some other one that brings the required used project as a part of it, be advised to confirm the usage manually.</p><p>For example, let’s say there are three unconfirmed usages from the project <em>RadioSet</em> to used projects <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances.</em> Instead of confirming each usage, you can create just one user-defined usage <em>RadioSet → ElectricComponents</em>, since the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> brings in <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances</em> as sub-used projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="972dce95-3005-401a-a44c-16d441e602d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>However, this is the case when you must be sure that the user-defined usage will be created in the right place.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="3bc31c71-f4de-46a6-86db-31aa78c7b3ff">In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Used Projects</strong> . The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>U</strong><strong>sed Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li data-uuid="a32fff48-640c-4df9-8c9f-b7c0509b2d61">Select a used project that is the usage target resource.</li><li data-uuid="28508039-800b-4123-a9b5-903ea78fa4e6">Click the <strong>Used project</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li data-uuid="247485cf-32c4-4d57-bdcb-2ef9464b3735">Follow the steps of the usual procedure (select a required used project and finish or continue to the second wizard step to provide more configuration options for used project usage and then finish). </li></ol><p><strong> </strong></p><p><strong>Rejecting usages</strong></p><p>If the usage A → B is not good according to the user policy, it must be rejected and removed.</p><p>You can reject an automated usage</p><ul><li data-uuid="8cef662e-ee8b-4d39-8cde-6cc7023db4e1">By using the specific command (for removing underlying model level references)</li><li data-uuid="d22ce084-35f0-4b20-b99b-6688d4f4ef3d"><span>Manually (by removing or redirecti</span>ng underlying model level references to different elements)</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To reject an automated usage by using the specific command</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d8a46b3-8f7e-4354-a8f0-b4e5c7e419f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Use this way to reject a model-level usage that, for example, was created inadvertently.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="3d1de5f8-7e39-4379-a619-fcde68bbe293">Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.</li><li data-uuid="9dbd1c23-281f-4036-a641-42b12ef1fc8e">Select <strong>Remove underlying model references</strong> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). All underlying model-level references (the ones that cause this automated usage) will be removed.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="045cf87d-638f-4e8b-a934-4e3b5b14322e"><ac:rich-text-body>This command is very similar to the <strong>Clear Recovered Element Usages</strong> command.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove an automated usage manually</p><hr /><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In more complex cases, you may want to address each usage individually. Use dependency analysis means to examine what dependencies are there and review them on a case-by-case basis (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Analyzing package dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependency matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and so forth).</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Useful commands for preparing to resolve unconfirmed used project usages</span></strong></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When you need to explore your project composition in detail before attempting to resolve unconfirmed usages, use the following commands for navigating:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="691670b4-51d1-4f27-bd44-0fd9dc009540"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></li><li data-uuid="38cc0c4b-18eb-4020-93b2-197dfdd1b22c">Open <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resouce Usage Map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for collaboration projects </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="c663c2e8-ddd5-4ee9-ae52-b036f38d3831"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Analyzing Usages and Dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="540cf722-5622-4e5f-aee0-3f6835102a01"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Analyzing Package Dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="652be26b-c44b-4a14-ada1-2001888d8174"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Using other Projects in a Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="0ad7bfed-fc6d-47e7-aa5e-5a7607d2eb38"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566924 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06105: Resource

- page_id: `249566924`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566924/Resource
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:23.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Organization Structure Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487916360

1487916360

1487916360

**Description**

The Resource is used to specify resources that can be referenced by Process. These Resources can be human resources as well as any other resource assigned to Activities during Process execution time.

Multiple processes can utilize the same Resource.

Resource can be element of the Organization structure such as Organization Unit, Role or Person.

1487916359

**Related elements**

- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system
- Pool and Lane
- Activities
- Choreography Activities

**Related diagrams**

- Organization Structure Diagram
- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram
- BPMN Resources Usage Matrices

**Related Procedure**

- Using Organization Structure Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564994 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06106: Resource Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `249564994`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564994/Resource+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:29.866+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Resource Assignment Wizard** is used to define resource for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

To open the **Resource Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''] (DoDAF), SV-8 (MODAF), NSV-8 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pr Configuration Management' space=''] (NAF 4.0), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space=''] (UAF)
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select Resource . The Resource Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resource selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newresource/ActualResource that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.

If you select or create new resource, the corresponding Actual Resource will be created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the Select Resource dialog switch on the Creation Mode .
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button, choose a resource category Security , Resources , or Business , and select the desired resource.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind InService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind OutOfService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong> is used to define resource for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.</p><p>To open the <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-8]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF), SV-8 (MODAF), NSV-8 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pr Configuration Management" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Pr]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Evolution diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Evolution]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF)</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select <strong>Resource</strong>.<br />The <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7906868e-8eaf-4211-b25d-81da0fd7f96b"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Resource selection</h3><p>In this step, you can select the existing or create new<span> resource/Actual </span>Resource that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed86cd71-43aa-4a8a-85bc-96c50b6951c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select or create new resource, the corresponding Actual Resource will be created automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a resource for the Version Of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Resource</strong> dialog switch on the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button, choose a resource category <strong>Security</strong>, <strong>Resources</strong>, or <strong>Business</strong>, and select the desired resource.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Start date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.</p><p>The Actual Project Milestone with Kind InService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d535bfaa-fd31-4790-942a-80eecc5bd7ca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting the day and time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step2.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>End date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.</p><p>The Actual Project Milestone with Kind OutOfService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2cdb224-4e8d-419f-b79d-f0ff69b67ebf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting the day and time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step3.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564999 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06107: Resource Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `249564999`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564999/Resource+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:30.340+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['resource-interaction-manager-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog enables the analysis and management of the Resource Exchange data. You can:

- Edit existing Resource Exchanges by adding new Resource Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Resource Exchanges on selected relationship
- Change the Resource Exchange Item [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_resource_exchange_item.png']
- Review producing and consuming Function pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review a list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open the **Resource Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Select Resource Connector, Association,Function Control Flow , Function Object Flow, or Resource Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_manager.png'] . The Resource Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Resource Exchange.
- The All Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the Resource Exchange Manager dialog.

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF' space=''].

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Resource Exchange Manager</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog enables the analysis and management of the Resource Exchange data. You can:</span></p><ul><li>Edit existing Resource Exchanges by adding new Resource Exchange Items</li><li>Realize or ignore existing Resource Exchanges on selected relationship</li><li>Change the Resource Exchange Item<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_resource_exchange_item.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Review producing and consuming Function pairs <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and remove the ones that are not needed</span></li><li>Review a list of <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Connector, Association,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Function Control Flow</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Function Object Flow, </span>or Resource Message in the diagram pane.</li><li>On the smart manipulator, click<ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange Manager" ac:alt="Resource Exchange Manager"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange_manager.png" /></ac:image>.<br />The <strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ac:alt="Resource Exchange Manager dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange_manager_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li>The <strong>Affected Diagrams</strong> button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Resource Exchange.</li><li>The <strong>All Affected Diagrams</strong> button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the <strong>Resource Exchange Manager </strong>dialog.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8618a39-c3c2-4266-a035-571ad94ca213"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968707 space=MT version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 06108: Resource Manager

- page_id: `243968707`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968707/Resource+Manager
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:44:14.589+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

7929296

INLINE

7929299

INLINE

319023586

**On this page**

**43**

7929297

INLINE

Resource Manager functionality allows you to manage local resources (installed with a modeling tool or downloaded separately) and the resources available on the server/shared repository.

With the Resource Manager, you can manage different types of resources, such as Profiles, Plugins, Templates, Language resources, Case studies/Samples, Custom diagrams, and others.

The Resource Manager functionality allows you to:

- Install the resources
- Update the resources
- Distribute needed resources internally (including the company's developed resources)
- Check dependency of a resource

The **Resource/Plugin Manager** helps you manage the resources. You can add the resources to your modeling tool from the following locations:

- Local file system/network share
- Webserver

##### Working with the Resource/Plugin Manager

To open the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog

- From the **Help** menu, select **Resource/Plugin Manager**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Resource/Plugin manager dialog.

To add resources from an *.rdzip* file on a local file system, network share, or a web server

The *core.rdzip* file containing all available resources is added automatically to the **Resource/Plugin Manager** by default.

1. In the Resource Location section, click the [...] button. The Manage Resource Locations dialog opens. Click one of the following:
  1. Add button to add an .rdzip file from a local file system or network share.
  2. Add URL button to add an .rdzip file from a web server. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. The file selection/URL dialog opens. Specify the location/URL of the *.rdzip* file and click **Open**.
3. Click OK . The resources of the file are loaded.
4. Select the check box of the resource you want to install.
5. Click the Download/Install button to install the selected resource. [ATTACHMENT filename='Install_a_plugin_through_the_pre-bundled_plugin_file.png']
6. Click Close and restart your modeling tool.

To add resources froma *.zip* file on a local file system or network share

1. Click the Import button. The file selection dialog opens.
2. Specify the location of the *.zip* file and click **Open**. The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.
3. Click **Close** and restart your modeling tool. 
**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

To browse added resource locations

1. In the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog, click on the Resource Location selection.
2. Select the resource location from the drop-down menu. The resources are added to the Resource/Plugin Manager from the added resource file. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_manager_location_drop-down.png']

To remove a resource location

1. In the Resource Location section, click the [...] button. The Manage Resource Locations dialog opens.
2. Select the resource location you want to remove.
3. Click the Remove button. Click OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

To uninstall a resource

1. Select the check box of the resource you want to uninstall.
2. Click the Remove button.
3. Click Close and restart the modeling tool. [ATTACHMENT filename='uninstall_a_resource.png']

To see the resource detailsand/or check the dependency of a resource

1. Select the check box of the resource whose details you want to view.
2. Click Details . The dialog expands with the resource description at the bottom. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_manager_details.png']
3. If the resource has dependencies, they are displayed in the Required compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_the_dependency_of_a_resource.png']

##### Adding resources from a file

The pre-bundled resource distribution file *core*.*rdzip* is bundled with every modeling tool. It is located at < *installation_directory>/data/resourcemanager/distribution* >. The file is automatically added to the **Resource/Plugin Manager**by default.

There are multiple ways to install resources using the **Resource/Plugin Manager**, which depends on your needs and preferences. See the image below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Visualization of the Resource/Plugin Manager workflow.

To learn more about plugin installation options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL'] page.

##### Distributing resources using *.rdzip*

Once you have the resource distribution file (*.rdzip*), you can share resources internally, e.g., on a company level.

The resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) contains the resources and their descriptor file. If you are using the web server for resource distribution, make sure that the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) is extracted into the intended server location.

##### Building your own custom resources file

You can bundle your resources into one *.rdzip* file and then distribute that file on a local file system, network share, or web server.

To build the resources file, you need to install the **Development Tools** plugin. The **Development Tools** plugincan be downloaded individually and then installed using **Resource/Plugin Manager**, or it can be found in the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*).

7929295

INLINE

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distributing custom diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distributing Resources' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distributing Profiles' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building a resource distribution file' space='']

914204988

914204989

The Resources (Rs) domain defines the solution architectures to implement operational requirements. It captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g. organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, natural resources.

914204987

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564398 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06110: Resources Connectivity

- page_id: `249564398`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564398/Resources+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:53.440+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914248742

914248744

914248743

**Description**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) table summarizes the exchanges between resources of information, systems, personnel, natural resources, etc., and the functions that produce and consume them. Measurements can optionally be included.

The Resources Connectivity Internal diagram addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.

The intended usage of the The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) view includes:

- Summarizing system resource exchanges.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.
- Detailed definition of resource flows.

**Implementation**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity matrix' space=''] . It represents exchanges between resources.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table' space=''] . It represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity Table' space='']
- Reports (.xlsx): - Resources Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Resources Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **An example of the Resources Connectivity diagram**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### An example of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Connectivity Table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources connectivity matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Role-based Connectivity Table

914248740

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity in UAF' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Edge' space='MED']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa8149f9-00f5-4d4f-bf13-ddcb81aa326e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">914248742</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c51821fd-c7fe-4710-90bc-7595b693f01d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">914248744</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96020ade-a8e9-4c38-9117-d34b9479e0fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">914248743</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) table summarizes the exchanges between resources of information, systems, personnel, natural resources, etc., and the functions that produce and consume them. Measurements can optionally be included.</p><p>The Resources Connectivity Internal diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.</span></p><p>The intended usage of the The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) view includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing system resource exchanges.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li><li>Detailed definition of resource flows.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Connectivity matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Connectivity matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It represents exchanges between resources.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Role-based Connectivity table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Connectivity Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Connectivity Table.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Reports (.xlsx):<br />- Resources Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.<br />- Resources Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_connectivity.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><strong>An example of the Resources Connectivity diagram</strong></h6><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_internal_connectivity.png" /></ac:image></strong></p><h6>An example of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource Connectivity" ac:alt="Resource Connectivity"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_connectivity.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Resources
````
