<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564425 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06111: Resources Constraints

- page_id: `249564425`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564425/Resources+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:56.278+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914505518

914505520

914505519

**Description**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) domain defines limitations, constraints, and performance parameters for resources, their interactions, performed functions, and data. It specifies traditional textual rules/non-functional requirements. The addition of SysML parametrics provide a computational means of defining resource constraints within a specific context.

The intended usage of the Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints table' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram' space=''] .
- Resources Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

914505517

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565004 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06112: Resources Forecast Creation Wizard

- page_id: `249565004`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565004/Resources+Forecast+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:30.835+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1622433108

1622433119

1622433109

The **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard** is used to create forecast tables.

The same wizard is used for the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NSV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NTV-2 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating TV-2 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='']

To open the **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Resources Forecast Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Diagram name and owning package specification

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Resources selection

In this step, choose the needed resources or create new resources. The selected resources will be represented as Resources Forecast table rows.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The resources (Capability Configuration, Competence, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Protocol, protocol Stack, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, Standard, System, Technology) are offered depending on the domain.

To create a resource

1. In the Select Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. From the list, select the resource type.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Actual Strategic Phases selection

In this step, you can select existing or create new Actual Strategic Phases which will be represented as Resources Forecast table columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Select Actual Strategic Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Strategic Phase, type its name.

1622433106

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NSV-9 table' space='']

/>The <strong>Resources Forecast Creation </strong><strong>Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7906868e-8eaf-4211-b25d-81da0fd7f96b"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Diagram name and owning package specification</h3><p>In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_1.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Resources selection</h3><p>In this step, choose the needed resources or create new resources. The selected resources will be represented as Resources Forecast table rows.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_2.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8550f47-2413-4909-b52c-3689c4a71ffe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The resources  (Capability Configuration, Competence, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Protocol, protocol Stack, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, Standard, System, Technology) are offered depending on the domain.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Resources</strong> step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>From the list, select the resource type.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Actual Strategic Phases selection</h3><p>In this step, you can select existing or create new Actual Strategic Phases which will be represented as Resources Forecast table columns.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_3.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Strategic Phase</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Strategic Phase, type its name.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5104d22-a3a4-4e2f-8aff-3bfb42843e2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1622433106</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating the SV-9 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-9 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NSV-9 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NSV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NTV-2 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NTV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating the SV-9 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MODAF. SV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating TV-2 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MODAF. TV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P1 Technology Forecast table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Technology Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating A8 Standards Forecast table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standards Roadmap" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Forecast table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Forecast diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564435 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06113: Resources Information

- page_id: `249564435`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564435/Resources+Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:57.299+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915064863

915064865

915064864

**Description**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) domain shows the information perspective on resource architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for logical and physical data modeling in the data model diagrams. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Information diagram' space=''] . It allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific or product-specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Information diagram' space=''] . It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space=''] . It supports the information engineering notation.

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Logical Data Model

915064862

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model Kind' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='']

It allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific or product-specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Information diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Physical Data Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Relationship Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564533 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06114: Resources Motivation

- page_id: `249564533`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564533/Resources+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:18.467+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Resources Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Resources Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Parametric diagram' space='']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564447 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06116: Resources Processes

- page_id: `249564447`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564447/Resources+Processes
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-07T13:23:25.223+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activities in support of capabilities. It describes the functions, their inputs/outputs, function actions and flows between them.

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The intended usage of the The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram' space=''] .
- Resources Processes Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Mission Engineering Threads diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space=''] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the process diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the process flow diagram

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity in UAF' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Condition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Control Flow' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Edge' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Object Flow' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Physical Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Architecture' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Item' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Parameter' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activities in support of capabilities. It describes the functions, their inputs/outputs, function actions and flows between them.</p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain addresses human and system functionality.</p><p>The primary purposes of The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain are to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="382ee30f-fd79-4a5b-9c2e-4b31d1c54755">Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.</li><li data-uuid="6734f749-116a-40fa-81ec-6dfd837e9a62">Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).</li><li data-uuid="3ff0c694-237a-42bf-af15-bb6591a47376">Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.</li></ul><p>The intended usage of the The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3eb82562-1e4a-4f00-8b8e-9c8b7b86b827">Description of task workflow.</li><li data-uuid="e4986306-d00d-4e93-9067-795c2babeb9a">Identification of functional system requirements.</li><li data-uuid="9cad3818-d142-4c36-9c6b-abf7b2997886">Functional decomposition of systems.</li><li data-uuid="b72146ce-19b4-4131-89d0-4b27220c9894">Relate human and system functions.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3f7c3ef8-1f6d-4333-b310-8c9c003e9da3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Processes diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="77a22ba5-e0b9-4742-a3fa-c264d5636d76"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="c8de15e0-4c48-4b43-9a9d-c814714f9303">Resources Processes Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">business </span>activities.</li><li data-uuid="af857ed6-fd27-451e-9766-24ad0337c8a6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Mission Engineering Threads diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mission Engineering Threads diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="cb4e8cbe-23d4-4320-bbf3-afe4441031ed"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource Processes" ac:alt="Resource Processes"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_processes.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the process diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_process_flow.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">An example of the process flow diagram</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f8dab7a7-8787-4bf4-ac0c-c3637a213c98"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Activity in UAF" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="49689ca7-36ad-455e-b497-0686a1b7a32c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Condition" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a209706b-424a-44cd-8bb3-5a01fbe078f0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Physical Resource" /></ac:link></li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564461 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 06117: Resources Roadmap

- page_id: `249564461`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564461/Resources+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:59.676+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917589031

917589033

917589032

**Description**

The **Resources Roadmap** (Rs-Rm) domain shows the resource structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

The Resources Roadmap (Rs-Rm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='']. It shows the resource structure changes over time. The intended usage of the Resources Evolution diagram includes:
  - Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
  - Planning technology insertion.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Forecast table' space='']. It shows the technology forecast. This diagram defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies. Expected supporting technologies are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements / trends. It provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The intended usage of the Resources Forecast table includes:
  - Forecasting technology readiness against time.
  - HR Trends Analysis.
  - Recruitment Planning.
  - Planning technology insertion.
  - Input to options analysis.
- Resources Evolution report (.docx).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources forecast

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources evolution

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564472 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06118: Resources Sequences

- page_id: `249564472`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564472/Resources+Sequences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:17.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915260432

915260434

915260433

**Description**

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) domain expresses the time-ordered examination of the interactions between resources (roles). Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) domain is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or resource role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Sequences diagram' space=''] that is based on interaction.
- Resources Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes an organization's sequence or flow of activities that show how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

915260431

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity in UAF' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Item' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Method' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) domain is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or resource role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Sequences diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Sequences diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that is based on interaction.</li><li>Resources Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes an organization's sequence or flow of activities that show how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource Interaction Scenario" ac:alt="Resource Interaction Scenario"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction_scenario.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="134b8b50-9d1a-411f-9cdc-6139e3a27b5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915260431</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Activity in UAF" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Method" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Role" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0cbbc21-6036-43ef-8033-80a4f4d40a2e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564480 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06119: Resources States

- page_id: `249564480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564480/Resources+States
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:01.705+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917825059

917825061

917825060

**Description**

The Resources States (Rs-St) domain shows the state-based behavior of a resource. It represents how the resource responds to various events and actions by changing the state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The intended usage of the The Resources States (Rs-St) domain includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

The Resources States (Rs-St) domain is represented by a Resource States diagram that is based on UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources States diagram

917825058

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource State Description' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564483 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06120: Resources Structure

- page_id: `249564483`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564483/Resources+Structure
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:02.157+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917923917

917923919

917923918

**Description**

The Resources Structure (Rs-Sr) domain references the resource structure, connectors and interfaces in a specific context. It defines the physical resources, e.g. capability configuration(s)/system(s) and interactions necessary to implement a specific set of Operational Performer(s). Can be used to represent communications networks and pathways that link communications resources and provides details regarding their configuration.

**Implementation**

The Resources Structure (Rs-Sr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space=''] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Structure diagram

917923916

It defines the physical resources, e.g. capability configuration(s)/system(s) and interactions necessary to implement a specific set of Operational Performer(s). Can be used to represent communications networks and pathways that link communications resources and provides details regarding their configuration.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564503 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06121: Resources Taxonomy

- page_id: `249564503`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564503/Resources+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:03.166+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918156776

918156778

918156777

**Description**

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of resources and the flows of resources among them.

The intended usage of the Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain includes:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- System resource flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain in represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space=''] . It addresses the composition and interaction of systems.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy table' space=''] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Taxonomy table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564521 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06122: Resources Traceability

- page_id: `249564521`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564521/Resources+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:18.048+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918383161

918383164

918383163

**Description**

The Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain shows the traceability between operational activities and functions that implements them. It depicts the mapping of functions to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful function performed by a resource or solution.

The intended usage of the Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

The Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space=''] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

918383160

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567463 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06123: Restriction

- page_id: `249567463`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567463/Restriction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:00.662+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To specify restriction, a generalization must be used between this class and a super class. This generalization either has or does not have the *XSDrestriction*stereotype. Restriction id and annotation maps to Generalization properties.

In order to have an inner simpleType element, the parent of this Generalization must be the inner Class of the outer UML Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Restriction model example.

#### PANEL: Restriction XML code sample

Restriction XML code sample

````html
<p>To specify restriction, a generalization must be used between this class and a super class. This generalization either has or does not have the <em>XSDrestriction </em>stereotype. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970783 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 06124: Retrieving Slot Values

- page_id: `243970783`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970783/Retrieving+Slot+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:58.218+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template variables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

126605373

126605376

126605371

**On this page**

32

126605376

Instead of using Stereotype and Tag, some projects may use Instance Specification and Slot to define new attributes of an element.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Instance Specification and Slot defining element attributes.

You can retrieve a slot value by either using a normal UML Specification structure, or by using a slot property.

#### Using normal UML Specification to Retrieve Slot Values

For example, to retrieve a Name slot value type:

```text
0)
				#set($v = $slot.value.get(0).text)
			#else
				#set($v = "")
			#end
			Slot value is $v
		#end
	#end
#end]]>
```

#### Using Slot Property to Retrieve Slot Values

You can use a slot property as a shortcut to get the following information from a slot.

##### Retrieving slot information from an element

The following code retrieves slot information from an element.

```text

```

In which:

- $element is an element.
- slots is a property for getting slot variables.

For example, if you want to obtain a collection of slot variables of an element, type:

```text

```

###### Retrieving slot information from an element and a classifier’s name

The following code retrieves slot information from an element and a classifier’s name.

```text

```

Where:

- $element is an element.
- slots is a property for getting the slots variable.
- ClassifierName is the classifier’s name.

The code returns:

- A list of slot variables whose classifier names are ClassifierName.
- If there are multiple classifiers that match the ClassifierName, the code returns the slot property of the first classifier.
- If there is no classifier that matches the ClassifierName, the code returns null.

For example, if you want to retrieve all slot values from the “Communicate” classifier, type:

```text

```

###### Retrieving slot value from an element, a classifier’s Name, and a defining feature name

```text

```

Where:

- **$element** is an element.
- **slots** is a property for getting slots variable.
- **ClassifierName** is the classifier’s name.
- **DefiningFeatureName** is the name of a slot.

The code returns:

- A slot value whose name is DefiningFeatureName and a classifier whose name is ClassifierName.
- If the slot multiplicity is < =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return the value in ValueSpecification.
- If the slot multiplicity is > 1, it will return the value in array of ValueSpecification.

For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a slot whose name is “Address” and those of a classifier whose name is “Communicate”, type:

```text

```

- ClassifierName and DefiningFeatureName are case-sensitive properties.
- You can use: $report.getSlotValue($element, ClassifierName, DefiningFeatureName) to retrieve the slot value. See $report below for further details.

##### Code examples to retrieve slot values

This section explains how to get the value of an instance slot from a classifier. The figure below shows the elements of a slot variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the element **Member** in the tree, or you can right-click on it and open the Specification dialog to see the values in the dialog.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
An example of elements in the Containment tree.

The following figure shows the Instance Specification dialog of the element Member of the slot variable. As you can see in the dialog, the element Member of the slot variable contains slot names and values.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
All slots of Instance Specification.

The following are examples of using the code to retrieve a value of a slot.

Example 1.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Address** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Communicate,**you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is :

```text

```

Example 2.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Telephone** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Communicate**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is > 1, [1..*].

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 3.

If you want to print the value of a slot Name of the element Member from the classifier Personal Data, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 4.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Nickname** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Personal Data**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is > 1, [0..*].

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 5.

If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot **Name** of the element **Member** from the classifier**Personal Data**, you can use the following template code.


The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 6.

If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot **Nickname** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Personal Data**, you can use the following template code.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d06e539-0d58-446f-833f-a51625c663e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="096acd2b-02ca-46a2-835c-c2a3d730d9bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4154f92b-3c0f-4bb0-be68-81013ec6f373"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605371</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf58dd29-bc69-4a3c-bf4d-8ece4bda8e35"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d39e1f93-7c8a-4fa2-a5f2-5690a286f3d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Instead of using Stereotype and Tag, some projects may use Instance Specification and Slot to define new attributes of an element.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ins_spec_slot.png" /></ac:image><br />Instance Specification and Slot defining element attributes.</h6><p>You can retrieve a slot value by either using a normal UML Specification structure, or by using a slot property.</p><h2>Using normal UML Specification to Retrieve Slot Values</h2><p>For example, to retrieve a Name slot value type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b4989e1-d02f-4ff1-b5ed-8b8ad7f9159a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
	#foreach($slot in $instance.slot)
		#if($slot.definingFeature.name == 'Name')
			#if($slot.value.size() > 0)
				#set($v = $slot.value.get(0).text)
			#else
				#set($v = "")
			#end
			Slot value is $v
		#end
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using Slot Property to Retrieve Slot Values</h2><p>You can use a slot property as a shortcut to get the following information from a slot.</p><h3>Retrieving slot information from an element</h3><p>The following code retrieves slot information from an element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b39cf1fe-654a-4b5e-b671-91481733c9d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In which:</p><ul><li>$element is an element.</li><li>slots is a property for getting slot variables.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to obtain a collection of slot variables of an element, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3eac2eb2-b0cc-47f3-9ed1-c8b041d4f4d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($slot in $instanceSpecification.slots)
	$slot.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Retrieving slot information from an element and a classifier’s name</h4><p>The following code retrieves slot information from an element and a classifier’s name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c455d57f-b3d8-4973-b2be-72be49ccc4b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots.ClassifierName
or
$element.slots.get("ClassifierName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where:</p><ul><li>$element is an element.</li><li>slots is a property for getting the slots variable.</li><li>ClassifierName is the classifier’s name.</li></ul><p>The code returns:</p><ul><li>A list of slot variables whose classifier names are ClassifierName.</li><li>If there are multiple classifiers that match the ClassifierName, the code returns the slot property of the first classifier.</li><li>If there is no classifier that matches the ClassifierName, the code returns null.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to retrieve all slot values from the “Communicate” classifier, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb72fff2-3360-41d6-ac20-1765a8cf551c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Retrieving slot value from an element, a classifier’s Name, and a defining feature name</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5432ff95-f5fb-4799-8564-bcc19ee954c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots.ClassifierName.DefiningFeatureName
or
$element.slots.ClassifierName.get("DefiningFeatureName")
or
$element.slots.get("ClassifierName").DefiningFeatureName
or
$element.slots.get("ClassifierName").get("DefiningFeatureName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Where:</p><ul><li><em><strong>$element</strong></em> <br />is an element.</li><li><em><strong>slots</strong></em> <br />is a property for getting slots variable.</li><li><em><strong>ClassifierName</strong></em> <br />is the classifier’s name.</li><li><em><strong>DefiningFeatureName</strong></em> <br />is the name of a slot.</li></ul><p>The code returns:</p><ul><li>A slot value whose name is DefiningFeatureName and a classifier whose name is ClassifierName.</li><li>If the slot multiplicity is &lt; =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return the value in ValueSpecification.</li><li>If the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, it will return the value in array of ValueSpecification.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a slot whose name is “Address” and those of a classifier whose name is “Communicate”, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bf54286-94b7-4664-a620-cf6d758e3dd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate.Address
$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate.get("Address")
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate").Address
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate").get("Address")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e75cb621-b6ac-4177-9752-236ea0dea673"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>ClassifierName and DefiningFeatureName are case-sensitive properties.</li><li><p>You can use:</p><pre>$report.getSlotValue($element, ClassifierName, DefiningFeatureName)</pre><p>to retrieve the slot value. See $report below for further details.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Code examples to retrieve slot values</h3><p>This section explains how to get the value of an instance slot from a classifier. The figure below shows the elements of a slot variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the element <strong>Member</strong> in the tree, or you can right-click on it and open the Specification dialog to see the values in the dialog.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="elem_tree.png" /></ac:image><br />An example of elements in the Containment tree.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows the Instance Specification dialog of the element Member of the slot variable. As you can see in the dialog, the element Member of the slot variable contains slot names and values.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="All Slots of Instance Specification" ac:alt="All Slots of Instance Specification"><ri:attachment ri:filename="insspec_slot.png" /></ac:image><br />All slots of Instance Specification.</h6><p>The following are examples of using the code to retrieve a value of a slot.</p><p><br /></p><p>Example 1.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Address</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Communicate, </strong>you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b922420-d26b-442a-bda9-f81a968391d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.Communicate.Address
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is :</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c2639a0-e006-4173-ab7d-371915f632d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is US]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 2.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Telephone</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Communicate</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, [1..*].</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="219a764c-2699-401d-a7e2-bdf9865f469c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.Communicate.Telephone
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea85fd57-1362-4570-91e9-26604f9f5efc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 3.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot Name of the element Member from the classifier Personal Data, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.   	Slot value is $instance.slots.get("Personal Data").get("Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db1e3d50-e33f-4396-b2fc-4cc92e690571"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Slot value is Peter]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Example 4.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Nickname</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, [0..*].</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="011960e3-c451-4bd2-972a-b0c80d35d260"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.get("Personal Data").get("Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba67d40e-fdee-48c3-ba22-15479f2c2610"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 5.</p><p>If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot <strong>Name</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier<strong> Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5202526-2083-47a6-b173-021d5420ae96"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $report.getSlotValue($instance, "Personal Data", "Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5170855-ce3d-472d-a861-c14836fe612f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [Peter]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 6.</p><p>If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot <strong>Nickname</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd460782-44cb-49a4-86ff-66ee419a0224"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $report.getSlotValue($instance, "Personal Data", "Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3217cea0-b93a-4f1b-9c06-ca007a8c966e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ Slot value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 7.</p><p>If you want to print the names and values of all classifiers and the slot properties of all instance elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae0ce5a7-fc3f-4c91-950b-a438ca841ce2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  #foreach($classifier in $instance.slots)
	Classifier name: $classifier.name
	Slots:
      #foreach($slot in $classifier)
	   $slot.name : $slot.value
      #end
 
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4225d8a6-12a2-4e96-a28b-386d75d8d4ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Classifier name: Personal Data
	Slots:
  	   Name : Peter
  	   Age : 20
  	   Nickname : [Peet, P]
  	   Gender : Male
  	   ID : 1234
 
	Classifier name: Communicate
	Slots:
  	   Telephone : [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]
  	   E-mail : [a@example.com, b@example.com]
  	   Address : US
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 06125: Retrieving tag values

###### An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree and all tags of a Class.

#### Using normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values

The sample code of usage of normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values is as follows:

```text
0)
        		#set($t = $tag.value.get(0))
        	#else
            	#set($t = "")
        	#end
        	Tag value is $t
        #end
	#end
#end]]>
```

#### Using tag properties to retrieve tag values

You can use tag properties as a shortcut to get the following information from tags.

##### Retrieving tag information from elements

The following code retrieves tag information from an element.

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.

For example, if you want to obtain a collection of tag properties of an element, type the following line:

```text

```

##### Retrieving tag information from elements and stereotype names

The following code retrieves tag information from an element and a stereotype name.

```text

```

or

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.
- StereotypeName is the name of a stereotype.

The code returns a list of tag variables whose tag stereotype names are **StereotypeName**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If there are multiple tag stereotype names that match StereotypeName , the code returns the tag property of the first tag stereotype name.
- If there is no tag stereotype name that matches StereotypeName , the code returns null.

For example, if you want to retrieve all tag values from the **Communicate** tag stereotype, type the following:

```text

```

`or`

```text

```

##### Retrieving tag values from elements, tag stereotype names, and tag names

Type one of the following lines:

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.
- StereotypeName is the name of a stereotype.
- TagName is the name of a tag.

The code returns the following:

- A tag value named TagName and a tag stereotype named StereotypeName .
- If the tag multiplicity is < =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return a value.
- If the tag multiplicity is > 1, it will return an array of values.

For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a tag named **Address** and those of a tag stereotype named **Communicate**, type the following lines:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**StereotypeName** and **TagName** are case-sensitive properties.

#### Code examples to retrieve tag values

This section explains how to get the value of a tag from a class. The figure below shows the elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the **Member** element in the tree, or you can right-click it and select **Specification** to open the **Specification** dialog to see the values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree.

The following figure shows the **Specification** dialog of the **Member** element of the tag variable. The **Member** element of the tag variable contains tag names and values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All tags of a Class.

The following code examples are for retrieving the value of a tag.

Example 1:

If you want to print the **Address** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Communicate stereotype,**you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity *=* *1*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 2:

If you want to print the **Telephone** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Communicate stereotype**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity is *> 1, [1..*]*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 3:

If you want to print the **Name** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Personal Data**stereotype, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity *=* *1*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 4:

If you want to print the **Nickname** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Personal Data stereotype**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity is *> 1, [0..*]*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 5:

If you want to print the names and values of all stereotypes and the tag properties of all class elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d26bfa2-8c6a-428d-81e6-900e0a79980f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887862</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5b88173-958f-4b73-a833-ff158e3bddbf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887866</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0f684f8-4123-4c24-a9d9-49a7a5b57f5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887858</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b48e59ae-c7c8-4a44-88df-4753946893e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ad8cc2-a627-499c-aec0-f35f887fc2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887865</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree and all tags of a Class.</h6><h2>Using normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values</h2><p>The sample code of usage of normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f051079-0cde-4e27-b32b-acee013cb77c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
	#foreach($tag in $class.taggedValue)
		#if($tag.tagDefinition.name == "Name")
			#if($tag.value.size() > 0)
        		#set($t = $tag.value.get(0))
        	#else
            	#set($t = "")
        	#end
        	Tag value is $t
        #end
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using tag properties to retrieve tag values</h2><p>You can use tag properties as a shortcut to get the following information from tags.</p><h3>Retrieving tag information from elements</h3><p>The following code retrieves tag information from an element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20a664f6-a7b1-449f-b897-bd9d19316188"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Given that:</p><ul><li><strong>$element</strong> is an element.</li><li><strong>tags</strong> is a property for getting the tag variable.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to obtain a collection of tag properties of an element, type the following line:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3437bad3-8dd8-4e06-bedb-3dc54d781b16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($tagProperty in $class.tags)
    $tagProperty.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Retrieving tag information from elements and stereotype names</h3><p>The following code retrieves tag information from an element and a stereotype name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85987d6e-0b26-48ed-a718-2514a8571703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags.StereotypeName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">or</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags.get("StereotypeName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Given that:</p><ul><li><strong>$element</strong> variables whose tag stereotype names are <strong>StereotypeName</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0705fca-f7c8-4502-b4b4-61273014f02e"><ac:parameter you want to retrieve all tag values from the <strong>Communicate</strong> tag stereotype, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62198b1e-fd1c-4e71-9c6e-c6c63b1c53d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$class.tags.Communicate]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><code>or</code></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ebe1aa26-e9db-4684-bcca-798a00ea3e3a"><ac:parameter names, and tag names</h3><p>Type one of the following lines:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ca6f495-f4d4-4909-9126-2cdbee889646"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags.StereotypeName.TagName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="654b8930-1c50-49e9-b908-2bb4460f2971"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags.StereotypeName.get("TagName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3cbf876-60e7-466b-a664-6c26af4d7eaf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags.get("StereotypeName").TagName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code"
````
