that:</p><ul><li><strong>$element</strong> is an element.</li><li><strong>tags</strong> is a property for getting the tag variable.</li><li><strong>StereotypeName</strong> is the name of a stereotype.</li><li><strong>TagName</strong> is the name of a tag.</li></ul><p>The code returns the following:</p><ul><li>A tag value named <strong>TagName</strong> and a tag stereotype named <strong>StereotypeName</strong>.</li><li>If the tag multiplicity is &lt; =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return a value.</li><li>If the tag multiplicity is &gt; 1, it will return an array of values.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a tag named <strong>Address</strong> and those of a tag stereotype named <strong>Communicate</strong>, type the following lines:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04ab7a36-15c2-46cb-9385-013fe578a6df"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$class.tags.Communicate.Address
$class.tags.Communicate.get("Address")
$class.tags.get("Communicate").Address
$class.tags.get("Communicate").get("Address")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c23bc4c-d4d2-4b96-a973-9db6f650f133"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>StereotypeName</strong> and <strong>TagName</strong> are case-sensitive properties.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Code examples to retrieve tag values</h2><p>This section explains how to get the value of a tag from a class. The figure below shows the elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the <strong>Member</strong> element in the tree, or you can right-click it and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog to see the values.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Elements of a Tag Variable in the Containment Tree.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree.</h6><p>The following figure shows the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog of the <strong>Member</strong> element of the tag variable. The <strong>Member</strong> element of the tag variable contains tag names and values.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="All Tags of a Class.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All tags of a Class.</h6><p>The following code examples are for retrieving the value of a tag.</p><p><br /></p><p>Example 1:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Address</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Communicate stereotype, </strong>you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity <em>=</em> <em>1</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2e0acf1-f137-4b64-b376-13067b01018d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.Communicate.Address
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84eed87e-6920-453d-b013-673b42886303"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is US]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 2:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Telephone</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Communicate stereotype</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity is <em>&gt; 1, [1..*]</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e632674-c73e-4b80-aa4c-7787c2b15ebc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.Communicate.Telephone
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="557721da-40ab-4d0b-b890-2374e914f29b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 3:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Name</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Personal Data </strong>stereotype, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity <em>=</em> <em>1</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eed33e1-343b-43bc-81e0-0e29999b3737"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.get("Personal Data").get("Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4997521f-3e20-4bf0-8df9-d56686eb0dfb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is Peter]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 4:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Nickname</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Personal Data stereotype</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity is <em>&gt; 1, [0..*]</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd2e623e-a161-4716-8579-656959277b4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.get("Personal Data").get("Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="412e8b35-348c-4ba7-9fc9-9daf722145dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 5:</p><p>If you want to print the names and values of all stereotypes and the tag properties of all class elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0eca2d30-f2cd-4d24-aff4-2c9fb8ce53f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
	#foreach($tagProperty in $class.tags)
    	Stereotype name: $tagProperty.name
    	Tags:
      		#foreach($tag in $tagProperty)
       			$tag.name : $tag.value
      		#end
  	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c598e4-9fe1-44f6-aac7-a615af43a442"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Stereotype name: Communicate
Tags:
	Address : US
	Telephone : [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]
	E-mail : [a@example.com, b@example.com]
Stereotype name: Personal Data
Tags:
 	Gender : Male
	ID : 1234
	Age : 20
	Name : Peter
	Nickname : [Peet, P]
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562193 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 06126: Reusing and referencing existing Modelica models

- page_id: `249562193`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562193/Reusing+and+referencing+existing+Modelica+models
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-08T14:53:16.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Modelica export
- labels: ['modelica-port', 'modelica-parameter', 'modelica-block', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The «ModelicaBlock», «ModelicaParameter» and «ModelicaPort»stereotypes are created to specify which SysML v1 properties (including ports) of its blocks correspond to Modelica library reusable component.The semantics of these elements are given by the corresponding elements in the Modelica libraries.For more details, readthe[OMG SysPhS specification](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/).

The figure below shows how the «ModelicaBlock», «ModelicaParameter» and «ModelicaPort» stereotypes are definedin the SysML profile.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Modelica stereotypes.

##### Using «ModelicaBlock» stereotype

Apply «ModelicaBlock» stereotype for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] to reference its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] withan equivalent component in the Modelica library.The value of the name tag must be a fully qualified name (e.g. Modelica.Electrical.Analog.Basic.Capacitor) of the corresponding component in the Modelica library. >]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The «ModelicaBlock» stereotype applied for a *Capacitor* Block and created referenceto corresponding component in the Modelica library.

##### Using «ModelicaParameter» stereotype

Apply «ModelicaParameter» stereotype for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'] to reference it with an equivalent parameter of a Modelica library.The stereotyped Value Property must be owned by a Block stereotyped by «ModelicaBlock». The value of the name tag is the name of the corresponding parameter, and the value attribute gives the value of this parameter. If the value is empty, the value of the parameter must be given using initial values of the stereotyped property.

In the following image, after the «ModelicaParameter» stereotype is applied for the *r* Value Property, its name tag value is referenced with the parameter *R* in the Modelica library. >]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The «ModelicaBlock» *Resistor* owns *r* Value Property stereotyped by «ModelicaParameter» and created reference tothe Modelica library component.

##### Using «ModelicaPort» stereotype

Apply «ModelicaPort» stereotype for a port to reference it with an equivalent port in the Modelica library. The stereotyped port must be owned by a Block with «ModelicaBlock» stereotype applied.The value of the name tag gives the name of the corresponding port in the Modelica library. >]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The «ModelicaBlock» *Ground* owns *p* Proxy Port stereotyped by «ModelicaPort» and created reference to the Modelica library component.

##### Referencing with Modelica library components

To reference SysML v1 elements with Modelica library components

1. Apply « ModelicaBlock », «ModelicaParameter» and «ModelicaPort» stereotypes respectively for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']>]]>
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], make sure the **Expert** or **All** mode is selected.
3. Select the **Tags** property group, make sure [IMAGE alt='' src=''] is switched off.
4. Select the *name* property and click **Create Value**.
5. Type the name of the corresponding component in the Modelica library as follows: 
 - for a Block type the fully qualified name, e.g. *Modelica.Electrical.Analog.Basic.Resistor*. 
 - for a Value Property type the parameter name, e.g. *R*. 
 - for a port type the port name, e.g. *p*.
6. Click **Close**. 
The SysML v1 elements are referenced with the component in the Modelica library.

The figure below shows a comparison between exported model without and with references to the Modelica library components. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modelica export' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562029 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06127: Reusing and referencing Simulink models

- page_id: `249562029`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562029/Reusing+and+referencing+Simulink+models
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:57:40.739+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export
- labels: ['simulink-port', 'simulink-block', 'simulink-parameter', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

##### Introduction

The «SimulinkBlock», «SimulinkParameter» and «SimulinkPort» stereotypes are created to specify which SysML v1 properties (including ports) of its blocks correspond to Simulinklibrary reusable component.The semantics of these elements are given by the corresponding elements in the Simulink libraries.For more details, readthe[OMG SysPhS specification](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/).

The figure below shows how the «SimulinkBlock», «SimulinkParameter» and «SimulinkPort» stereotypes are defined in the SysML profile.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulink stereotypes.

##### Using «SimulinkBlock» stereotype

Apply the «SimulinkBlock» stereotype for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] to reference its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] with an equivalent component in Simulink. The value of the name tag must be a type of a Simulink Block (e.g. "Trigonometry").

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The «SimulinkBlock» stereotype is applied to the *Sin*Block and a reference is created to the corresponding component in the Simulink library.

You can also reference an already existing Simulink Model. To do that, set the "name" tag to "ModelReference", and the "url" tag to the name of the Simulink file.

When exporting a SysML element to a Simulink file, you can have the <<SimulinkBlock>> stereotype automatically applied to the Block. In such a case, the "name" and the "url" tags are automatically filled. When a part of this Block is exported, a reference to an already exported Simulink file is created instead of a full part export.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using «SimulinkParameter» stereotype

Apply the «SimulinkParameter» stereotype for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'] to reference it with an equivalent parameter of a Simulink library. The stereotyped Value Property must be owned by a Block stereotyped by «SimulinkBlock». The value of the name tag is the name of the corresponding parameter, and the value attribute gives the value of this parameter. If the value is empty, the value of the parameter must be given using the initial values of the stereotyped property. If the name tag is empty, the property name will be used instead.

In the following image, the «SimulinkParameter» stereotype is applied for the "operator" Value Property under the "Sin" Block described in the previous section. This property defines which Trigonometry operator is used on Simulink.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The «SimulinkBlock» *Sin*owns *operator* Value Property stereotyped by «SimulinkParameter» with a reference tothe Simulink library component.

##### Using «SimulinkPort» stereotype

Apply the «SimulinkPort» stereotype for a port to reference it with an equivalent port in the Simulink library. The stereotyped port must be owned by a Block with the «SimulinkBlock» stereotype applied. The value of the name tag gives the name of the corresponding port in the Simulink library.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The«SimulinkBlock»*Ground*owns*p*Proxy Port stereotyped by «SimulinkPort» with a reference to the Simulink library component.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The value of the name tag must be a type of a Simulink Block (e.g. &quot;Trigonometry&quot;).</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SimulinkBlock.PNG" /></ac:image></span>The «SimulinkBlock» stereotype is applied to the <em>Sin </em>Block and a reference is created to the corresponding component in the Simulink library.</h6><p>You can also reference an already existing Simulink Model. To do that, set the &quot;name&quot; tag to &quot;ModelReference&quot;, and the &quot;url&quot; tag to the name of the Simulink file.</p><p>When exporting a SysML element to a Simulink file, you can have the &lt;&lt;SimulinkBlock&gt;&gt; stereotype automatically applied to the Block. In such a case, the &quot;name&quot; and the &quot;url&quot; tags are automatically filled. When a part of this Block is exported, a reference to an already exported Simulink file is created instead of a full part export.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SimulinkExport.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h3>Using «SimulinkParameter» stereotype</h3><p>Apply the «SimulinkParameter» stereotype for a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Property" /></ac:link> to reference it with an equivalent parameter of a Simulink library. The stereotyped Value Property must be owned by a Block stereotyped by «SimulinkBlock». The value of the name tag is the name of the corresponding parameter, and the value attribute gives the value of this parameter. If the value is empty, the value of the parameter must be given using the initial values of the stereotyped property. If the name tag is empty, the property name will be used instead.</p><p>In the following image, the «SimulinkParameter» stereotype is applied for the &quot;operator&quot; Value Property under the &quot;Sin&quot; Block described in the previous section. This property defines which Trigonometry operator is used on Simulink.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SimulinkParameter.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The «SimulinkBlock» <em>Sin </em>owns <em>operator</em> Value Property stereotyped by «SimulinkParameter» with a reference to </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Simulink library component.</span></h6><h3>Using «SimulinkPort» stereotype</h3><p>Apply the «SimulinkPort» stereotype for a port to reference it with an equivalent port in the Simulink library. The stereotyped port must be owned by a Block with the «SimulinkBlock» stereotype applied. The value of the name tag gives the name of the corresponding port in the Simulink library. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SimulinkPort.png" /></ac:image></span><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">«SimulinkBlock» </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><em style="text-align: center;">Ground</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> owns </span><em style="text-align: center;">p</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Proxy Port stereotyped by «SimulinkPort» with a reference to the Simulink library component.</span></span></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565322 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06128: Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation

- page_id: `249565322`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565322/Reusing+Measurement+Sets+for+Actual+Property+Set+creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:37.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: ['reuse-measurement-sets-for-actual-objects', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a <viewpoint name>* Role, you can quickly reuse the measurements of their type (Asset). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Property Sets is displayed in the Specification window of the element for which you are creating the Actual Property Set.

You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurements or Measurement Sets when creating the following Actual Property Set:

- <viewpoint name>* Role

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The type measurements in the Actual Measurements compartment are displayed *Italic*.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Resources, Services, Strategic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Role, you can quickly reuse the measurements of their type (Asset). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Property Sets is displayed in the Specification window of the element for which you are creating the Actual Property Set.</p><p>You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurements or Measurement Sets when creating the following Actual Property Set:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;*</span> </span>Role</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reusing measurements structure owned by roles type.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83d4c9a2-d883-498c-b5ad-54430acd2301"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type measurements in the Actual Measurements compartment are displayed <em>Italic</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>- Operational, Resources, Services, Strategic.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=246156554 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06129: Reverse Options

- page_id: `246156554`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/246156554/Reverse+Options
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:39.168+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Code engineering
- labels: ['java-packages', 'round-trip-set', 'importing-packages', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Demo**

- Code Reverse

The reverse is the opposite of code generation. Existing code can be converted to UML models with the help of the modeling tool reverse feature. In order to perform a reverse operation,prepare the sets the same way you did for code generation, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space=''].

- Choose Reverse from the Code engineering sets item shortcut menu.
- Choose Reverse from the selected set shortcut menu.

The UML model for the component can be reversed in the same way. Simply select the component you are interested in from the browser and click Reverse on its shortcut menu. Models can be reversed without creating a set.

To reverse a model without creating a set

1. In the main menu, click **Tools** >**Code Engineering** > **Quick Reverse**and then choose from one of the needed options. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space='']**Round Trip Set** dialog opens. This menu item is unavailable from System Analyst and System Engineer perspectives.
2. Select the files from the Round Trip Set dialog box, Add Files tab.
3. Click OK . The Reverse Options dialog box appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='reverse_options.png']

The following table outlines Dialog descriptions:

| Area | Element name | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Create class fields as | Attributes | fields are represented in a model as . |
| Associations | Class fields are represented in a model as . |  |
| According to rules | or Attribute creation on the reverse is the ability to enter that help decide if an association or attribute must be created on reverse. |  |
| Resolve collection generics | Reverse engineering can create associations when one class has a collection of other classes. If selected, types of collections will be resolved (property type will not be a collection, but a real type). |  |
| Reset already created fields | Select this option if you want to keep the previously created UML representation (attribute or association) for class fields. |  |
| Model refresh type | Merge Model and Code | The model elements are updated by code. Elements that do not exist in the code will not be removed from the model. |
| Change Model According to Code | The model will be created strictly by code. Entities in the model that do not match entities in the code will be discarded. |  |
| Vizualization | Visualize reserved model | Classes created while reversing can be added to a diagram. |
| Launch Model Visualizer | After reversing, the Model Visualizer dialog box appears. |  |
| Create new Class Diagram | After reversing, the Create Diagram dialog box appears. Create a new diagram where the created entities will be added. |  |
| Add to active diagram | After reversing, all created entities will be added to the currently opened diagram. |  |
| Analysis.Create dependencies between | Classifiers | Dependencies between classes will be analyzed and created. |
| Packages | Dependencies only between packages will be created. |  |

Method bodies are not parsed during dependency search. Only static information is used.

If you have a code set combined from several files, you may see changes you wish to model without reversing all the code. Only changed files should be reversed. You can do this type of reversing by clicking the **Refresh** button on the set shortcut menu, or by performing a model refresh from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space=''].

**Related Pages**:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Existing code can be converted to UML models with the help of the modeling tool reverse feature. In order to perform a reverse operation, </span>prepare the sets the same way you did for code generation, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering Sets" /></ac:link>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="50963574-a245-44c9-ae2f-21106d325a6c">Choose<strong> Reverse</strong> from the <strong>Code engineering sets</strong> item shortcut menu.</li><li data-uuid="4a49bb88-c89c-4676-b7df-edb96d28d75e">Choose<strong> Reverse</strong> from the selected set shortcut menu.</li></ul><p>The UML model for the component can be reversed in the same way. Simply select the component you are interested in from the browser and click Reverse on its shortcut menu. Models can be reversed without creating a set.</p><p><br /></p><p>To reverse a model without creating a set</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c62f963f-3f02-453f-ac62-bca945f35c03"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Code Engineering</strong> &gt; <strong>Quick Reverse </strong>and then choose from one of the needed options. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968243 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06130: Reversing Information Flow direction

- page_id: `243968243`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968243/Reversing+Information+Flow+direction
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:08:30.267+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can reverse the direction of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MED'] on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'], as shown in the following example. After reversing the direction, the flow directions are updated in all the related diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The direction of *Coffee* Information Flow is reversed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

To reverse Information Flow direction

1. Select the Connector that has the Information Flow set in the Containment tree or on the diagram pane.
2. From its shortcut menu, select Refactor > Reverse Flow Direction . The direction of the Information Flow is changed and updated in all the related diagrams.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can reverse the direction of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Flow" /></ac:link> on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, as shown in the following example. After reversing the direction, the flow directions are updated in all the related diagrams.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reverse_flow_direction.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The direction of <em>Coffee</em> Information Flow is reversed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6><p>To reverse Information Flow direction</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Connector that has the Information Flow set in the Containment tree or on the diagram pane.</li><li>From its shortcut menu, select <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Reverse Flow Direction</strong>.<br />The direction of the Information Flow is changed and updated in all the related diagrams.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7fdd9a-3dbd-4e01-84bb-0fecdc173a0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Flow" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968238 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06131: Reversing relationships direction

- page_id: `243968238`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968238/Reversing+relationships+direction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:54.131+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The direction of the particular relationship can be reversed. You can reverse the direction of the transition, control flow, object flow, and other relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The relation can be reversed if:

- The selected relationship and elements connected by this relationship are editable.
- The source and target elements are compatible, that is, they can be counterchanged.

To reverse a relationship direction

1. Select the relationship in the Model Browser or the relationship’s path on the diagram pane.
2. From the element’s shortcut menu, select Refactor > Reverse Direction . The direction of the relationship changes.

The direction of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MED'] will not be changed on the direction reverse action. Additionally, in some cases when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MED'] is not valid anymore, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MED'] will be removed from the project after the direction reverse action.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can reverse the direction of the transition, control flow, object flow, and other relationships.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of direction reverse" ac:title="Example of direction reverse"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reverse_direction.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The relation can be reversed if:</p><ul><li>The selected relationship and elements connected by this relationship are editable.</li><li>The source and target elements are compatible, that is, they can be counterchanged.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To reverse a relationship direction</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the relationship in the Model Browser or the relationship’s path on the diagram pane.</li><li>From the element’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Reverse Direction</strong>. The direction of the relationship changes.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2d7cd84-30e7-4e62-a865-5ffb8a643fd3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The direction of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[information flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> will not be changed on the direction reverse action. Additionally, in some cases when the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[information flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is not valid anymore, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[information flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> will be removed from the project after the direction reverse action.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da1d48d6-ef32-4ca2-80de-08f61840eddb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 06132: Risk

- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1986204347

1986204349

1986204348

**Description**

A Risk is a category of Impact Value that indicates the impact and probability of loss. Some Risks are expressible as formulas, for example:

- Probability of loss (for example, 5% probability)
- Potential loss (for example, $500,000 loss)
- Unit-of-measure (for example, loss in USD)

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1986204346

**Related elements**

- Assessment Concepts
- Assessment Concept Relationships

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966606 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06133: Robustness diagram

- page_id: `243966606`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966606/Robustness+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:06.591+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

373764986

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

373764988

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

373764987

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Robustness Analysis involves analyzing the narrative text of use cases, identifying a first-guess set of objects that participate in those use cases and classifying the objects based on the roles they play.

- Boundary or Interface objects are what actors use in communicating with the system.
- Entity objects are mostly objects from the domain model.
- Control objects (known as controllers because they often are not real objects) serve as the "glue" between boundary objects and entity objects.

The Robustness Analysis acts as a preliminary design within the project lifecycle, providing the missing link between an analysis and a detailed design.

Four basic rules apply:

1. Actors can only talk to the boundary objects.
2. The boundary objects can only talk to the controllers and actors.
3. The entity objects can only talk to the controllers.
4. The controllers can talk to the boundary objects and entity objects, and to other controllers, but not to the actors.

Both the boundary objects and entity objects are nouns; the controllers are verbs. Nouns cannot talk to other nouns, but verbs can talk to either nouns or verbs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the Robustness diagram *Logging In*

373764985

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extension diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2be67fb6-4331-41f2-9560-7c4f3f146570"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">373764986</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="432cefea-25c9-43ee-8936-299dac8e8e49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">373764988</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0071d02c-b9f5-426f-b5ca-695c4342c483"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">373764987</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce52db42-5307-42b7-bb41-0ae23699d8ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Robustness Analysis involves analyzing the narrative text of use cases, identifying a first-guess set of objects that participate in those use cases and classifying the objects based on the roles they play.</p><ul><li>Boundary or Interface objects are what actors use in communicating with the system.</li><li>Entity objects are mostly objects from the domain model.</li><li>Control objects (known as controllers because they often are not real objects) serve as the &quot;glue&quot; between boundary objects and entity objects.</li></ul><p>The Robustness Analysis acts as a preliminary design within the project lifecycle, providing the missing link between an analysis and a detailed design.</p><p>Four basic rules apply:</p><ol><li>Actors can only talk to the boundary objects.</li><li>The boundary objects can only talk to the controllers and actors.</li><li>The entity objects can only talk to the controllers.</li><li>The controllers can talk to the boundary objects and entity objects, and to other controllers, but not to the actors.</li></ol><p>Both the boundary objects and entity objects are nouns; the controllers are verbs. Nouns cannot talk to other nouns, but verbs can talk to either nouns or verbs.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-13 at 12.07.56 PM.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of the Robustness diagram <em>Logging In</em></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52511bf1-358a-4c85-900c-540aa8e272a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">373764985</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extension diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extension diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ad1d5667-7503-48cd-bd11-ffd59bf9b032" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

1488025783

1488025786

1488025785

**Description**

A Role is a type of contact point or a responsible person, e. g., cashier, ticket seller counter, etc. Roles may belong to an organization. This element is combined with the Resource Role definition.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1488025782

- Organization Unit
- Person
- Information system
- Pool and Lane
- Activities
- Choreography Activities

- BPMN Process Diagram

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561419 space=MT version=11 -->
## PAGE 06135: Rollup Pattern Wizard

- page_id: `249561419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561419/Rollup+Pattern+Wizard
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2025-09-08T19:06:03.686+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['rollups', 'rollup-pattern', 'pattern', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Rollup calculations are among the most common use cases in systems engineering. System modelers want to calculate total mass, cost, power, or another system dimension, based on individual values of all the parts in the model. The pattern can recursively propagate the particular value up a hierarchy of components characterized by this value.

The **Rollup Pattern** **Wizard** allows you to calculate the total cost, mass, power, or another system metric of elements in the model. Different types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Rollup Pattern Block' space='']pattern Blocks can be created to suit the calculation needs. This new modeling tool provides three [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space=''] that calculate the total cost, mass, or power. You can apply the default or newly created pattern Block by using the **Rollup Pattern** **Wizard**. It automatically [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Rollup Pattern Blocks' space=''] (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='']) to any number of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] recursively, and creates property values. This wizard also allows you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Rollup Pattern Blocks' space=''] from the selected Block or Instance Specification. The procedures below describe how to open and use the **Rollup Pattern Wizard**.

##### Opening the Rollup Pattern Wizard

To open the **Rollup Pattern Wizard**

1. From the shortcut menu of the Block or Instance Specification to which you want to apply a pattern Block, select:****
  - **Tools** > **Apply Rollup Pattern**, if you want to apply a pattern Block for the selected Block or Instance Specification.
  - **Tools** > **Remove Rollup Pattern**, if you want to remove the pattern Block from the selected Block or Instance Specification.

##### The Rollup Pattern Wizard areas

The **Rollup Pattern Wizard**consists of two areas:

- Select/Remove Pattern Block (1).
- Options (2).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Rollup Pattern Wizard when trying to apply a rollup pattern for a Block. Two areas of the wizard are highlighted: 1- Select Pattern Block, 2 -Options.

The procedures below describe how to work with the **Rollup Pattern Wizard:**

- 
- 

##### select_blockSelecting/ removing the pattern Block

To select/remove the pattern Block

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt_down.png'] to expand the list of available pattern Blocks.
2. Choose the rollup pattern Block that you want to apply/ remove.

##### select_optionsSelecting the options

| Apply Rollup Pattern Box | Description | Option appears when applying rollup pattern for... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Apply Recursively | Applies the pattern Block recursively. | Block, Instance Specification |
| Set Role Name | Sets the role names of Part Properties. | Block |
| Set Subsetted Properties | Sets the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties. | Block |
| Create Value Properties and Redefine | Creates and redefines Value Properties. | Block |

| Remove Rollup Pattern Box | Description | Option appears when applying rollup pattern for... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Recursively Remove Rollup Pattern | Removes the pattern Block recursively. | Block, Instance Specification |
| Remove Role Name | Removes the role names of Part Properties. | Block |
| Remove Subsetted Properties | Removes the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties. | Block |
| Remove Value Properties | Removes Value Properties. | Block |

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970822 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06136: Rotating images

- page_id: `243970822`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970822/Rotating+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.650+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050254487

2050254501

2050254485

**On this page**

43

2050254491

There are two rotating methods that you can use to rotate a given image by 90 degrees: (i) **rotateRight** for clockwise rotation and (ii) **rotateLeft** for counterclockwise rotation.

###### **$image.rotateRight(image)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The rotated image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.rotateLeft(image)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The rotated image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.rotateRight($diagram.image)**, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees clockwise. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image rotate right function.

Use **$image.rotateLeft($diagram.image)**, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees counterclockwise. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image rotate left function.

RTF Image rotation, with the scaling quality set to 'highest,' is not supported in any RTF document when opened with [OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=246156571 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06137: Round Trip

- page_id: `246156571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/246156571/Round+Trip
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-01T13:17:56.140+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Code engineering > Global options for Code Engineering
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Round Trip feature keeps your code and model synchronized. Because Round Trip traces all model and code changes, you can freely change entity specifications without discarding code changes made outside the tool.

For example, Round Trip prevents a job from being damaged by code editions or changes when the following steps are performed:

1. A Class Base is created within the tool.
2. The Operation getInstance is added to the Class.
3. Code is generated.
4. With an external tool, a programmer adds some code to that operation.
5. With a modeling tool, a name of the operation is changed to Instance .
6. Code is generated.

If the tool rewrites the whole code, these changes are made without corrupting the programmer’s job. The name of the operation is changed, but the internals remain the same.

Round Trip catches all changes in your project and controls the following actions:

- If the source code is not changed, you are not allowed to refresh the UML model. The Refresh command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable.
- If the model is changed but the code remains the same (new members were added or their headers were changed), refresh is not allowed, and the Refresh command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable. When generating code according to changes, all changes in the model are written to the signatures of class members, leaving the old implementation in place.
- If the code is changed but the model remains the same, a refresh can be executed; the code will reverse to the UML models. If the Code Generation Options dialog box appears when you attempt to generate code, you can select a code action that differs from the UML model.
- I f the code and model are changed while refreshing, all changes in the code are treated as new items and added to the model.
- If data in the model file is deleted, it will be restored while refreshing, even when the code has not been changed or the data itself is unimportant.

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global options for Code Engineering' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code engineering' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Round Trip feature keeps your code and model synchronized. Because Round Trip traces all model and code changes, you can freely change entity specifications without discarding code changes made outside the tool.</p><p>For example, Round Trip prevents a job from being damaged by code editions or changes when the following steps are performed:</p><ol><li data-uuid="6471ba42-2867-42c0-ac1a-43c508643cb2">A Class <em>Base</em> is created within the tool.</li><li data-uuid="f20fef1c-fa0e-4e0e-bfe2-70f61693f203">The Operation <em>getInstance</em> is added to the Class.</li><li data-uuid="7fb24a81-8228-4ad4-a65b-2dbfe5bfb3ad">Code is generated.</li><li data-uuid="d4fb44af-9eb2-48af-bf26-559d4169f78a">With an external tool, a programmer adds some code to that operation.</li><li data-uuid="ec185d5e-11d9-4e00-8598-6c4afbbfed5e">With a modeling tool, a name of the operation is changed to <em>Instance</em>.</li><li data-uuid="433b713a-754b-4f0d-9009-58bfb89ba891">Code is generated.</li></ol><p>If the tool rewrites the whole code, these changes are made without corrupting the programmer’s job. The name of the operation is changed, but the internals remain the same.</p><p>Round Trip catches all changes in your project and controls the following actions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5d4d78ab-9c93-4e0c-85ce-0b4b50a0474f">If the source code is not changed, you are not allowed to refresh the UML model. The <strong>Refresh</strong> command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable.</li><li data-uuid="d0233e2f-67b0-4451-9ef5-a91fdcd9476f">If the model is changed but the code remains the same (new members were added or their headers were changed), refresh is not allowed, and the <strong>Refresh</strong> command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable. When generating code according to changes, all changes in the model are written to the signatures of class members, leaving the old implementation in place.</li><li data-uuid="7e6cf991-7590-4e3b-9e8f-6747c598dd52">If the code is changed but the model remains the same, a refresh can be executed; the code will reverse to the UML models. If the <strong>Code Generation Options</strong> dialog box appears when you attempt to generate code, you can select a code action that differs from the UML model.</li><li data-uuid="ebb2641c-227c-4e25-be49-d7eb40915726"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">I</span>f the code and model are changed while refreshing, all changes in the code are treated as new items and added to the model.</li><li data-uuid="faf0d111-1e11-47f0-a041-946c64228ac8">If data in the model file is deleted, it will be restored while refreshing, even when the code has not been changed or the data itself is unimportant.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ce106958-b2cc-43fc-b69e-a24c7bc6d31d"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global options for Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2571bc6d-81a9-4890-b937-dd03baf03e2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567343 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06138: Routines

- page_id: `249567343`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567343/Routines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:28:01.771+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Database support > Database modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

125674665

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

125674667

125674663

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

125674666

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Routines example.

SQL supports several different kinds of routines. There are global routines, that are not bound to a particular type but belongs to the schema. There are two kinds of these routines - Procedures and Functions. And there are routines, that are bound to a particular structured user defined type - Methods. Each routine kind can have several parameters. Parameters have type and direction (**in**, **out**, **inout**). Functions and methods in SQL have return types - this is formalized in UML models by having an additional parameter with **return**direction kind.

There is an UML limitation, that UML does not allow to place UML operations (which are used to model SQL procedures and functions) directly in the UML packages (which are used to model SQL schemas). For this reason global routines are placed into a special container class - GLOBALS (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Top level elements' space='']).

Routines can be external (written in some other languages and attached to database engine) or SQL routines. In the latter case, body of the routine can be specified in the model. Due to UML specifics, there are two ways to specify the routine body - by filling the UML **method**field or by filling the UML **bodyCondition**field of the operation. These two ways are visible in the Specification window under the field names **Source (as method)**and **Source (as body condition)**. When specifying routine body, specify only one of these fields.

To use “as method” way

1. Right-click the GLOBALS element in the Containment tree and from its shortcut menu select New Element > Source . A source element (a UML OpaqueBehavior with the «Source» stereotype applied) under the GLOBALS element will be created in your schema.
2. In the Specification window of routine, edit the Source (as method) property value and in the opened dialog select the source element you’ve just created.

To use “as body condition” way, you simply have to fill the field. The routine body model element (in this case, UML Constraint - holding UML OpaqueExpression) shall be created under your routine model element.

Besides the standard SQL element properties, all 3 kinds of routines have the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specific Name | Additional name for the routine, uniquely identifying it throughout the system. |
| Deterministic | Specifies whether routine is deterministic (always gives the same output with the same data input). |
| Parameter Style | SQL or GENERAL. |
| SQL Data Access | Specifies how routine accesses SQL data (NO SQL \| CONTAINS SQL \| READS SQL DATA \| MODIFIES SQL DATA). |
| Source (as method) | Fields holding routine body text (choose only one). |
| Source (as body condition) |  |
| Creation TS | Routine creation and last edit timestamps. |
| Last Altered TS |  |
| Authorization ID | Authorization identifier which owns this routine (owner of the schema at routine creation time). |
| Security | Determines the authorization identifier under which this routine runs. Typically set to “INVOKER”, “DEFINER”, “IMPLEMENTATION DEPENDENT”. |
| External Name | The name of the external language routine implementation method (if routine is non-SQL routine). |

##### Procedure

#### NOTE: Note

Note

SQL Procedure is modeled as UML Operation with «Procedure» stereotype applied. For the sake of compactness, procedures are displayed with the «proc» keyword (instead of the long form - «Procedure») on the diagram.

Procedure is an operation that can be SQL-invoked and performs some actions depending on the parameters supplied. Procedures are global for schema - they are created under the GLOBALS model element.

Besides the standard properties of SQL routines (see ), procedure has the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Max Result Sets | If result set count is returned by procedure is dynamic, this value limits count thereof (DYMANIC RESULT SETS <max> clause). |
| Old Save Point | Savepoint level indicator for procedure (false means that new savepoint must be established before the procedure is run.). |

##### Function

#### NOTE: Note

Note

SQL Function is modeled as UML Operation with «Function» or «BuiltInFunction» or «UserDefinedFunction» stereotype applied. By default the «UserDefinedFunction» is used, however if another kind can be freely used if it is necessary for modeling needs (e.g. if we are modeling some built in library and want to specify that functions are built-in and not user defined).

For the sake of compactness, functions are displayed with the «func» keyword (instead of the long form) on the diagram.

Function describes some operation that calculates and returns some value depending on the parameters supplied. Functions are global for schema - they are created under the GLOBALS model element.

Besides the standard properties of SQL routines (see ), function has the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Null Call | Specifies that function returns NULL when called with NULL parameter value (RETURNS NULL ON NULL INPUT clause). |
| Type Preserving | Specifies that function does not change the type of the supplied parameter (returns the same object). |
| Transform Group | Allows to specify TRANSFORM GROUP <groups> clause - single or multiple. |

##### Method

#### NOTE: Note

Note

SQL Method is modeled as UML Operation with «Method» stereotype applied. For the sake of compactness, methods are displayed with the «func» keyword (instead of the long form - «Method») on the diagram.

Method is a function of the structured user defined type. It is created inside the structured UDT.

Besides the properties of SQL functions (see ), method has the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Constructor | Specifies that function is a constructor (used to construct values of the enclosing structured UDT). |
| Overriding | Specifies that function is overriding the same-named function from the parent structured UDT. |

##### Parameter

#### NOTE: Note

Note

SQL Parameter is modeled as UML Parameter with «Parameter» stereotype applied.

This model element specifies data inputs / outputs into routine calculations. Parameter has a type, direction (in / out / inout for usual parameters and a single parameter with direction return for functions) and default value.

Besides the standard SQL element properties, parameter has the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Type | Type of the parameter. |
| Type Modifier |  |
| Default Value | Default value (used when value is not supplied during routine invocation). |
| Direction | Direction of data flow through the parameter (into the routine, out of the routine or both). |
| Locator | AS LOCATOR modifier of the type. Specifies that instead of value, means to locate value are transferred. |
| String Type Option | Only valid when parameter type is XML type. Specifies underlying string datatype. |
| Cast Type | Additional options, specifying that return parameter is cast from another type (possibly with locator indication). |
| Cast Locator |  |

##### Cursor and routine result table

#### NOTE: Note

Note

SQL Cursor is modeled as UML Parameter with the «Cursor» stereotype applied.

When routine does not return a scalar value but a collection of the table values, cursor is used, instead of the parameter. Cursor has a type. This type must be some table type instead of the scalar types used for parameters. It can be an actual table / view from the model, if the cursor returns values from that table, or (if cursor returns data from some SELECT statement) can be a synthetic table. A Routine Result Table model element is used for this purpose (UML Class with «RoutineResultTable» stereotype applied). Its modeling is exactly the same as the normal tables - this is just an ephemeral table.

Besides the standard SQL element properties, cursor has the following properties available in the Specification window.

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Type | Type of the cursor. Should point to the routine result table. |
| Type Modifier |  |
| Direction | Direction of data flow through the parameter (into the routine, out of the routine, routine result). |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967282 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06139: Routing paths

- page_id: `243967282`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967282/Routing+paths
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:53.725+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['route-paths-rectilinearly', 'route-paths', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To route paths using the **Route Paths Rectilinearly** button, do either

- Select a path on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='route_paths_rectilinearly.png'] . The selected path is routed rectilinearly.
- Select a shape on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='route_paths_rectilinearly.png'] . All the paths that are connected to the shape are routed rectilinearly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To route paths using the <strong>Route Paths Rectilinearly</strong> button, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a path on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Route Paths Rectilinearly" ac:alt="Route Paths Rectilinearly"><ri:attachment ri:filename="route_paths_rectilinearly.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The selected path is routed rectilinearly.</li><li>Select a shape on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Route Paths Rectilinearly" ac:alt="Route Paths Rectilinearly"><ri:attachment ri:filename="route_paths_rectilinearly.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. All the paths that are connected to the shape are routed rectilinearly.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Routing paths rectilinearly" ac:alt="Routing paths rectilinearly"><ri:attachment ri:filename="routing_paths_rectilinearly.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06140: Row and column element shortcut menu

- page_id: `243966567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966567/Row+and+column+element+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:05.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open a row/column element shortcut menu

- Right-click the appropriate row/column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Command (Shortcut key) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specification | Opens the element’s . |
| Select in Containment Tree | Selects the element in the of the . |
| Go To | Finds and navigates to a diagram which is owned by the selected element, or to the one, wherein the element shape is drawn, or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship.For more information, see, |
| Related Elements | Opens the dialog for defining options to search for specified usages or dependencies of the selected element.For more information, see . |
| Refactor | the selected element to an element indicated by the user. |
| Tools | Opens a list of that are available for the selected element. |
| Up (Ctrl+Up Arrow) | Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) up. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows. |
| Down (Ctrl+Down Arrow) | Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) down. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows. |
| Left (Ctrl+Left Arrow) | Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) left. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns. |
| Right (Ctrl+Right Arrow) | Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) right. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns. |
| Sort Order | Point to the command and select the order for sorting rows or columns. It can be either Ascending or Descending. Selecting Custom does not affect the order of rows/columns. The Custom order is automatically selected after shifting at least one row or column to from one place to another.The sort order persists after changing the row or column owner's display mode, which can be defined by the Row Owner Display Mode or Column Owner Display Mode property in the matrix . |
| Remove from Matrix | Removes the selected element from the matrix. |
| Delete | Deletes the selected element from both the matrix and the model.If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To open a row/column element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the appropriate row/column.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Shortcut menu of Dependency Matrix row element" ac:alt="Shortcut menu of Dependency Matrix row element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dep_matrix_short_menu_row.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Command (Shortcut key)</p></th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Specification</strong></td><td>Opens the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong></p></td><td>Selects the element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Go To</strong></td><td><p>Finds and navigates to a diagram which is owned by the selected element, or to the one, wherein the element shape is drawn, or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship.<br />For more information, see</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element usage in diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Inserting hyperlinks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>,</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Traceability properties in Go To submenu" /></ac:link></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Related Elements</strong></td><td><p>Opens the dialog for defining options to search for specified usages or dependencies of the selected element.<br />For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Refactor</strong></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Refactoring" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Converts or replaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the selected element to an element indicated by the user.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tools</strong></td><td>Opens a list of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tools" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tools]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that are available for the selected element.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Up (Ctrl+Up Arrow)</strong></p></td><td><p>Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) up. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Down (Ctrl+Down Arrow)</strong></p></td><td><p>Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) down. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Left (Ctrl+Left Arrow)</strong></p></td><td><p>Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) left. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Right (Ctrl+Right Arrow)</strong></p></td><td><p>Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) right. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Sort Order</strong></td><td><p>Point to the command and select the order for sorting rows or columns. It can be either Ascending or Descending. Selecting Custom does not affect the order of rows/columns. The Custom order is automatically selected after shifting at least one row or column to from one place to another.<br />The sort order persists after changing the row or column owner's display mode, which can be defined by the <strong>Row Owner Display Mode</strong> or <strong>Column Owner Display Mode</strong> property in the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove from Matrix</strong></td><td>Removes the selected element from the matrix.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Delete</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Deletes the selected element from both the matrix and the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1245abe4-7d08-47ee-8303-2b836b84e1bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=246156562 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06141: Rules of association or attribute creation on reverse

- page_id: `246156562`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/246156562/Rules+of+association+or+attribute+creation+on+reverse
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:39.911+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Code engineering > Reverse Options
- labels: ['managing-association', 'managing-attribute', 'define-rules', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319165

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319167

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319166

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space=''] creation on the reverse is the ability to enter rules to help decide if an association or attribute must be created on reverse.

To create association or attribute creation rules on reverse

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select the code engineering set for reverse.
2. Right-click the set and choose the Reverse command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reverse Options' space='']**Reverse Options** dialog opens.
3. In the Create class fields as area, select According to rules and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button. The Class Field Creation Rules dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='class_field_creation_rules.png']
4. Click Add to add a rule.

Managing association or attribute creation on reverse rules

The **Class Field Creation Rules** dialog box lists the described rules. If a type qualified name matches any rule, a specified type is created. Rules described in this dialog are executed in order from top to bottom. If one rule matches, no others are executed. The dialog allows you to add a new rule, edit or remove an old one, and reorder rules.

- If no rules are matched, an attribute or association is created according the radio button choice at the bottom of the dialog.
- Click Add to add an expression.
- Click Edit to edit a selected rule.

To define rules

You can define rules in the **Rules** dialog. Click the **Add** or **Edit** button in the **Class Field Creation** dialog to open this dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Rules** dialog box description:

- In the Create drop-down list, choose Attribute or Association on reverse if a property matches a defined criterion.
- The Qualified Name text box supports simple search patterns, as well as '*' and '?' symbols.
- The Qualified Name pattern can contain a qualified name of the target element including a separator of a UML separator style (::). The qualified name is counted to the first package with the «ModelLibrary» stereotype.
- The Element Type drop-down list contains a subset of UML Type names: Any, Class, Interface, DataType, Primitive, Enumeration, Stereotype values are displayed.
- If the <UNSPECIFIED> value is selected in the Element Type drop-down list, the element type is matched by the qualified name expression.

#### TIP: Sample of the association creation on reverse

Sample of the association creation on reverse

These two samples show how to create associations among reversed classes, while avoiding creating associations to the used libraries (java).

If you place your classes inside the package “*com::myProduct*”, you must follow these Creation rules:

- Create Association, if the property type qualified name matches “ com::myProduct::* ” with “ all ” element type
- If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.

If you place your classes inside two packages “*myClient*” and “*myServer*”, the Creation rules should be:

- Create Association , if the type name “match” path “ myClient::* ” with an <UNDEFINED> meta type.
- Create Association , if the type name “match” path “ myServer::* ” with an <UNDEFINED> meta type.
- If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.

419319163

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reverse Options' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code engineering' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563586 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06143: S1 Service Taxonomy

- page_id: `249563586`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563586/S1+Service+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:20.120+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s1-service-taxonomy', 's1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

68648442

68648444

68648443

**Description**

The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.

A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.

The S1 view may be used to:

- SOA governance.
- Identification of services.
- Service planning.
- Service audit.
- Service gap analysis.
- Providing reference services for architectures.
- Tailoring generic services for specific applications.

**Implementation**

The S1 view can be represented using:

- S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1.png']
- C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1_matrix.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space=''] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

68648441

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']

A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.</span></p><p>The S1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>SOA governance.</li><li>Identification of services.</li><li>Service planning.</li><li>Service audit.</li><li>Service gap analysis.</li><li>Providing reference services for architectures.</li><li>Tailoring generic services for specific applications.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S1 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML
