<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563617 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06144: S2 Service Internal Structure

- page_id: `249563617`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563617/S2+Service+Internal+Structure
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:16.186+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create an S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Create Service Roles .
2. Associate Service Roles using Service Connectors.
3. Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.

##### Creating Service Exchange in S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create a Service Exchangein the S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange.png'] and then click the Service Connector.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Service Exchangedata.

##### Showing or hiding realized Service Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the Service Exchange Item on the element realizing Service Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized Service Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] :
  1. If theService Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='services_show_hide.png'] Show Realized Service Exchanges . Click it to hide the Service ExchangeItem.
  2. If the Service ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide Realized **Service Exchanges** . Click it to hide the Service ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one Service Exchange, the Service ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Service Exchanges, the Service Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose Service Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose Service ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_service_exchanges.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating S2 Service Internal Structure diagram</h3><p>To create an S2 Service Internal Structure diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Roles</span>.</li><li>Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Roles</span> using Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span><span> </span></li><li><span>Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.</span></li></ol><h3><span>Creating Service Exchange in S2 Service Internal Structure diagram</span></h3><p>To create a Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in the S2 Service Internal Structure diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange.png" /></ac:image>and then click the Service Connector.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Showing or hiding realized Service Exchanges</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can choose to either show or hide the Service Exchange Item on the element realizing Service Exchanges.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the realized Service Exchange Items</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the<span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span></span></span>Item is not displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, the tooltip for the button is</span><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_show_hide.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Show Realized Service Exchanges</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click it to hide the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item</span> is displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, the tooltip for the button is<span> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_hide.png" /></ac:image></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Hide Realized <strong>Service Exchanges</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Click it to hide the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item.<br />If the<span> </span>element realizes only one Service Exchange, the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.<br /></span></li></ol></li><li>If the element realizes multiple Service Exchanges, the Service Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:<ol><li>Select the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item you want to be displayed.</li><li><p>Clear the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Service Exchange </span>Item you want to be hidden.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fe22c9d-e07f-4265-afb2-8ceeecce5e43"><ac:rich-text-body>To select all check boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Select All<span> </span></strong>button. To clear all check boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Clear All<span> </span></strong>button.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_service_exchanges.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563599 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06145: S2 Service Structure

- page_id: `249563599`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563599/S2+Service+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:59.707+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S2 Viewpoint identifies and specifies how services are structured to create higher-aggregated services. To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.

**Implementation**

The S2 view can be represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the S2 Service Structure diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The S2 Viewpoint identifies and specifies how services are structured to create higher-aggregated services. To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The S2 view can be represented using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ S2 Service Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Sample</strong> </span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">An example of the S2 Service Structure diagram.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563637 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06146: S3 Service Interfaces

- page_id: `249563637`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563637/S3+Service+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:00.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s3-service-interfaces', 's3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

68833919

68833921

68833920

**Description**

The purpose of the S3 Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.

**Implementation**

The S3 view can be represented using:

- S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S3.png']
- S3 MS Word document report.

68833918

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='MED']

A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.</p><p>Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="S3 Service Interfaces diagram" ac:alt="S3 Service Interfaces diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S3.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="114de996-cdfe-4074-886b-5de4d3ff8db8">S3 MS Word document report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea61458a-ddf2-42c6-9d88-b9e40ab0a244"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">68833918</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Property" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Parameter" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Port" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Port" /></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563640 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06147: S4 Service Functions

- page_id: `249563640`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563640/S4+Service+Functions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:16.565+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s4-service-functions', 's4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824179280

824179282

824179281

**Description**

The S4 Service Functions view defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. S4 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space=''] and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

**Implementation**

The S4 view can be represented using:

- S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4.png']

- S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4_flow.png']
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- UML Class diagram.

824179278

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Control Flow' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Object Flow' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563650 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06148: S5 Service States

- page_id: `249563650`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563650/S5+Service+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:16.927+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s5-service-states', 's5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824386959

824386961

824386960

**Description**

The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The S5 view can be represented using a S5 Service States diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824386958

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service State Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S5 view can be represented using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee4b8256-c894-4fc6-aa98-222af27fda31">S5 Service States diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="S5 Service States diagram" ac:alt="S5 Service States diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S5.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c07d1543-7678-4bab-bd8f-d00b4602d54a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824386958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563653 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06149: S6 Service Interactions

- page_id: `249563653`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563653/S6+Service+Interactions
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-22T13:28:21.600+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s6-service-interactions', 's6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='']). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L6 - Logical Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="S6 Service Interactions diagram" ac:alt="S6 Service Interactions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S6.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="498e9ba7-f994-4713-8f7a-14e5ca7a1014"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-orange-bolder,#c25100);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="32f498e1-4b0e-4711-a84f-fb440c2de3b6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Message" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="ffb8ca8f-27dc-4227-9fbb-814de950a499"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f5f20156-b7e5-4c2a-936f-d7b0d2a0e2bf"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Property" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- page_id: `249563656`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824520441

824520443

824520442

**Description**

The S7 Service I/F Parameters view identifies all the parameters used in Service Interfaces (see also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S3 Service Interfaces' space='']).

**Implementation**

The S7 view can be represented using S7 Service I/F Parameters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824520440

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563664 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06151: S8 Service Parametric

- page_id: `249563664`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563664/S8+Service+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:17.323+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498484258

498484260

498484259

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specifiction. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498484257

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3480db-fc7b-4a03-a43a-b23d31d83517"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484258</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b862fb5-b94e-4498-8552-9b333d962dca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484260</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9889853a-21ca-4dc2-846e-ce9969bc4bac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484259</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Service Specifiction. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67eb6d9b-397a-4e8d-9f70-f4bd54e72298"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484257</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563659 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06152: S8 Service Policy

- page_id: `249563659`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563659/S8+Service+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:01.186+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s8-service-policy', 's8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824966407

824966409

824966408

**Description**

The purpose of the S8 Service Policy view is to specify constraints that apply to implementations of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.

**Implementation**

The S8 view can be represented using:

- S8 Service Policy table. [ATTACHMENT filename='S8.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S8 Service Parametric' space=''] diagram.
- S8 spreadsheet report .

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

824966406

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Policy' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562258 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06153: Sample models

- page_id: `249562258`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562258/Sample+models
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:49:30.225+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1552880298

1552880298

1552880298

Below, a list of example cases are provided to show the usability of the Modelica/Simulink export plugin for SysML v1 modeling and simulation of physical interactions signal flows. Each example contains a brief description of the example as well as capabilities of the plugin that the example attempts to display.

1. Humidifier System Example: Humidifying a Room
  1. The purpose of this example is to show how a SysML v1 model of a humidifier system, one that is trying to control the humidity in a room, can be created with state machines and signal flows. It is important to note that the signal flows between system components are unidirectional, and physical conservation laws do not apply in these applications because the same signal can be sent to multiple receiving components. In some cases, the state machines determine a timing or event trigger for when a signal flows between components. [ATTACHMENT filename='humidifier_scheme.png']
  2. Capabilities: Modeling & simulation of signal flows; exports SysML state machine diagrams into state machines in Simulink’s Stateflow environment.
2. Circuit Example: Voltage Source, Resistors, Inductor, Capacitor
  1. The purpose of this example is to show how a simple SysML v1 model involving the flow of electric charge (current) from a voltage source to resistors, an inductor, and a capacitor can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape using the SysML extension plugin for signal flow and physical interaction. Though the documentation should lead to generating Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files from the SysML model, the already exported Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files are provided in the package as examples. [ATTACHMENT filename='electric_circuit_scheme.png']
  2. Capabilities: Modeling & simulation of physical interactions; exports the SysML plugin's properties into flow and effort variables for Simulink’s Simscape toolbox (electrical domain).
3. Signal Processor Example: From Source to Sink
  1. The purpose of this example is to show a SysML v1 model of a signal processor, where a signal is sent from a function generator to be conditioned by an amplifier, frequency filters, and a mixer. [ATTACHMENT filename='signal_processor_scheme.png'] This example portrays the signal flows between system components. The signal flow is unidirectional, and physical conservation laws do not apply in these applications because the same signal can be sent to multiple receiving components. In this model, the function generator is being amplified and then sent to parallel filters (one high-pass filter and one low-pass filter), until the mixer combines the filtered signals for the final 
signal processor output.
  2. Capabilities: Modeling & simulation of signal flows.
4. Hydraulics Example: Two Tanks & A Pipe
  1. The purpose of this example is to show how a simple SysML v1 model involving the flow of a fluid between two tanks can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape using the SysML extension plugin for signal flow and physical interaction. Though the documentation should lead to generating Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files from the SysML v1 model, the already-exported Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files are provided in the package as examples. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Capabilities: Modeling & simulation of physical interactions; exports the SysML v1 plugin properties into flow and effort variables for Simulink’s Simscape toolbox (mechanical domain).

1552987985

**Related pages:**

****

In some cases, the state machines determine a timing or event trigger for when a signal flows between components. <br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="humidifier_scheme.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Capabilities: Modeling &amp; simulation of signal flows; exports SysML state machine diagrams into state machines in Simulink’s Stateflow environment. </li></ol></li><li><strong>Circuit Example: Voltage Source, Resistors, Inductor, Capacitor</strong><ol><li>The purpose of this example is to show how a simple SysML v1 model involving the flow of electric charge (current) from a voltage source to resistors, an inductor, and a capacitor can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape using the SysML extension plugin for signal flow and physical interaction. Though the documentation should lead to generating Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files from the SysML model, the already exported Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files are provided in the package as examples.<strong><br /></strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="electric_circuit_scheme.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Capabilities: Modeling &amp; simulation of physical interactions; exports the SysML plugin's properties into flow and effort variables for Simulink’s Simscape toolbox (electrical domain). </li></ol></li><li><strong>Signal Processor Example: From Source to Sink</strong><ol><li>The purpose of this example is to show a SysML v1 model of a signal processor, where a signal is sent from a function generator to be conditioned by an amplifier, frequency filters, and a mixer. <strong><br /></strong><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="signal_processor_scheme.png" /></ac:image><br /><p>This example portrays the signal flows between system components. The signal flow is unidirectional, and physical conservation laws do not apply in these applications because the same signal can be sent to multiple receiving components. In this model, the function generator is being amplified and then sent to parallel filters (one high-pass filter and one low-pass filter), until the mixer combines the filtered signals for the final<br />signal processor output.</p></li><li><p>Capabilities: Modeling &amp; simulation of signal flows.</p></li></ol></li><li><strong>Hydraulics Example: Two Tanks &amp; A Pipe</strong><ol><li><p>The purpose of this example is to show how a simple SysML v1 model involving the flow of a fluid between two tanks can be exported into Modelica or Simulink/Simscape using the SysML extension plugin for signal flow and physical interaction. Though the documentation should lead to generating Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files from the SysML v1 model, the already-exported Modelica and Simulink/Simscape files are provided in the package as examples.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hydraulics_scheme.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Capabilities: Modeling &amp; simulation of physical interactions; exports the SysML v1 plugin properties into flow and effort variables for Simulink’s Simscape toolbox (mechanical domain).</p></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0ca7214-69af-474d-bef3-bd9f8a32e610"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1552987985</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="065f6bcd-70d6-4af3-964e-39653a36a029" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968702 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06154: Sample of the Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `243968702`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968702/Sample+of+the+Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:07.940+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This sample describes step-by-step instructions how to create profile migration mapping rules and perform transformation. In this sample we will change one stereotype to other.

1. Create Book stereotype with Class metaclass.
2. Create your model, for example, create Source package with Source Class diagram. Draw the Sample Class and apply the Book stereotype. To change the Book stereotype to other, for example to Magazine stereotype, you have to create profile migration transformation mapping rules. Follow next steps for creating the mapping.
3. Create the stereotype Magazine with Class metaclass.
4. From the Magazine stereotype to the Book stereotype draw Dependency relationship.
5. Apply ReplaceStereotype stereotype to the Dependency relationship. Profile Migration transformation mapping rule is created. Now you can start transformation.
6. To start the transformation, from the Tools menu, select Model Transformations . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Model Transformation Wizard' space=''] opens.
7. Select the Profile Migration transformation and click Next .
8. Select the Source package in the Select source/destination step. Click Next .
9. The From and To fields display the mappings of the selected transformation profile in the Check mappings step. Click Finish .

After this transformation stereotype of Sample Class will be changed to Magazine stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This sample describes step-by-step instructions how to create profile migration mapping rules and perform transformation. In this sample we will change one stereotype to other.</p><ol><li>Create <em>Book</em> stereotype with Class metaclass.</li><li>Create your model, for example, create <em>Source</em> package with <em>Source Class</em> diagram. Draw the <em>Sample Class</em> and apply the <em>Book</em> stereotype.<br />To change the <em>Book</em> stereotype to other, for example to <em>Magazine</em> stereotype, you have to create profile migration transformation mapping rules. Follow next steps for creating the mapping.</li><li>Create the stereotype <em>Magazine</em> with <em>Class</em> metaclass. </li><li>From the <em>Magazine</em> stereotype to the <em>Book</em> stereotype draw Dependency relationship.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype to the Dependency relationship.<br />Profile Migration transformation mapping rule is created. Now you can start transformation.</li><li>To start the transformation, from the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Transformations</strong>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Model Transformation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Transformation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Profile Migration</strong> transformation and click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Select the <em>Source</em> package in the <strong>Select source/destination</strong> step. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>The <strong>From</strong> and <strong>To</strong> fields display the mappings of the selected transformation profile in the <strong>Check mappings</strong> step. Click <strong>Finish</strong> .</li></ol><p>After this transformation stereotype of Sample Class will be changed to Magazine stereotype.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Profile Migration Transformation mapping" ac:alt="Profile Migration Transformation mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="profile_migration_transformation1.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967341 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06155: Save As Image dialog

- page_id: `243967341`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967341/Save+As+Image+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.936+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Saving as image
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Save As Image** dialog allows you to save one or multiple diagrams of your model as images. Also, you can save only the selected symbols instead of an entire diagram, and choose the desired image format.

To open the **Save As Image** dialog

- From the File menu, select Save as Image .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The view of the Save As Image dialog when the Selected diagrams option button is selected.

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active diagram | Option button | Select to save as image the currently active diagram. The active diagram name is displayed in brackets next to the option button name. |
| Selected symbols | Option button | Select to save as image specific symbols that are selected in the diagram. The option is active only if one or multiple symbols are selected in the diagram. |
| Selected diagrams | Option button | Select the option in order to save as image the selected diagrams in the model tree or diagram list. |
| Select diagrams | Text box | The text box is available only if the Selected diagrams option button is selected.In the Select diagrams box, type the name of the diagram you want to find. Search results are displayed as a model tree or a plain list right under the text box. Click the Tree or List tab in the search result area to switch to the desired view, and select the diagram(s) you want to save as image(s).To optimize your search, use . The Apply Filter check box and the filter options button are located below the search result area. |
| Image File / Working Directory | Text box | Select the working directory in which the image will be saved. |
| Image Format | Drop-down list | Select the image format. |
| Options | Button | Click to open the where you can specify image export properties, such as image resolution and exported image size. The properties provided in the Properties dialog depend on the selected image format. |
| Overwrite existing files | Check box | Select to overwrite already saved image files. |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<strong> Save As Image</strong> dialog allows you to save one or multiple diagrams of your model as images. Also, you can save only the selected symbols instead of an entire diagram, and choose the desired image format.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Save As Image</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Save as Image</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Save As Image dialog" ac:alt="Save As Image dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_image.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The view of the Save As Image dialog when the Selected diagrams option button is selected.</h6><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Dialog element</th><th colspan="1">Element type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Active diagram</strong></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select to save as image the currently active diagram. The active diagram name is displayed in brackets next to the option button name.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Selected symbols</strong></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select to save as image specific symbols that are selected in the diagram. The option is active only if one or multiple symbols are selected in the diagram.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Selected diagrams</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select the option in order to save as image the selected diagrams in the model tree or diagram list.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Select diagrams</strong></td><td colspan="1">Text box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The text box is available only if the <strong>Selected diagrams</strong> option button is selected.</p><p>In the <strong>Select diagrams</strong> box, type the name of the diagram you want to find. Search results are displayed as a model tree or a plain list right under the text box. Click the <strong>Tree</strong> or <strong>List</strong> tab in the search result area to switch to the desired view, and select the diagram(s) you want to save as image(s).</p><p>To optimize your search, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[search modes and scope filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box and the filter options button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png" /></ac:image> are located below the search result area.</p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Image File / Working Directory</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Text box</td><td>Select the working directory in which the image will be saved.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Image Format</strong></td><td colspan="1">Drop-down list</td><td>Select the image format.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td colspan="1">Button</td><td><p>Click to open the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties dialog" /></ac:link></strong> where you can specify image export properties, such as image resolution and exported image size. The properties provided in the <strong>Properties</strong> dialog depend on the selected image format.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Overwrite existing files</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td>Select to overwrite already saved image files.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving as image" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968128 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06156: Saving an Import Map

- page_id: `243968128`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968128/Saving+an+Import+Map
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T10:09:30.745+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Import Map is a single instance of mapping between Excel or CSV file data and exported elements. Once you saved the Import Map, you can reuse it when importing data by using a single map or a group of maps. Also, you can override an existing Import Maps instead of creating new ones if changes are required. As an example, in the image below, the new *CarBreak Requirements Import* map is saved under the *Excel/C**SV i**mports* Package. This Package is created automatically when saving your first Import Map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The CarBreak requirements, structure and satisfy relationship Import Maps saved under the *Excel/CSV imports* package in the **Save Import Map** dialog.

##### Saving a new Import Map

To save an Import Map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog specify mandatory options. You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
4. Click the Save Map button. The Save Import Map dialog opens.
5. In the **Tree** tab select the location to save a map. We highly recommend saving Import Maps to an Excel/CSV imports Package.
6. Click the Create button.
7. In the Specification window, type a map name in the Name property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='import_map_specification.png']
8. Click Close .
9. Click OK . Your map is saved under the selected Package.

##### Overriding an existing Import Map

To override an existing Import Map

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map .
2. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select the existing Import Map that you want to override.
3. Click OK .
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog change mapping options.
5. Click the**Save Map** button. The **Save Import Map** dialog opens.
6. Select an Import Map you want to override. By default, the Import Map you chose to load in #2 step is selected in the **Tree** tab.
7. Click OK .

The Import Map is overridden.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- Managing Map Groups
- Excel and CSV Import dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967340 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06157: Saving as image

- page_id: `243967340`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967340/Saving+as+image
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can save an image as an active diagram, selected symbols, or selected diagrams.

Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the following formats:

- Enhanced Metafile Format (*.emf) (supports language specific symbols)
- Encapsulated PostScript (*.eps)
- Joint Photographic Experts Group (*.jpg, *.jpeg)
- Portable Network Graphics (*.png)
- Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)
- Tagged Image File Format (*.tif, *.tiff)
- Windows Metafile Format (*.wmf)

To save an image

1. Open the diagram you want to save as an image. If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.
2. On the main menu, select File > Save as Image . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='']**Save As Image** dialog opens.
3. In the dialog:
  - Select whether to save an **Active diagram**, **Selected symbols**, or **Selected diagrams**. Selected diagrams**Selected diagrams** allow you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] in your model.
  - Specify the directory you want to save the image to.
  - Select the image format. Click Options [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space=''] according to the selected format.
4. Click Save . The image is saved in the directory you have specified.

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space=''] page to learn more.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']Diagramming
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space=''] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.</li><li>On the main menu, select <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save as Image</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Save As Image dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Save As Image</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the dialog:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select whether to save an <strong>Active diagram</strong>, <strong>Selected symbols</strong>, or <strong>Selected diagrams</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d6f1f2-7008-40ac-aa9b-912d4c92a548"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Selected diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Selected diagrams</strong> allow you to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[search for a particular diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specify the directory you want to save the image to.</li><li>Select the image format. Click <strong>Options </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[to specify image options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> according to the selected format.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>. The image is saved in the directory you have specified.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="519fb3a1-8e91-45c4-8872-407c6de756da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965886 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06158: Saving projects

- page_id: `243965886`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965886/Saving+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The native modeling tool format is **.mdzip* and **.mdxml*. You can also save projects as **.xml* and **.xml.zip*.

To save changes for later sessions, projects must be saved. While saving, you can edit the name of the project and its file format.

To save the project

1. From the File menu, select Save Project or Save Project As . Alternatively, you can click the Save button on the main toolbar or press the shortcut keys Ctrl + S. The Save dialog opens.
2. Select the destination directory (where you want the project to be saved) and type the chosen file name. The default project saving location is set according to your operating system:**Windows**: My Documents/**Linux**: /home/<username>/**OS X**: Users/Documents/

3. Select the format for saving a project:

- 
  - Packed MagicDraw File Format (**.mdzip*) (default). **.mdzip* is a MagicDraw native format. It contains various parts of the project compressed. You should select it if you intend to work with the project only in the modeling tool's environment.
  - MagicDraw File Format (**.mdxml*). You should choose it if you intend to analyze the project's structure, xml, or for other purposes. You can open *.mdxml projects with other UML tools as well.
  - XML (**.xml*). It is the same file format as **.mdxml*, only that **.mdxml* files can be opened with a modeling tool, whereas **.xml* files can only be opened with an XML editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If the Create Backup File check box is selected in the Environment Options dialog, the modeling tool automatically creates a backup file of the previously saved project. The backup is held in a file with a name identical to that of the project.
- If you want to save maximum additional information to an *.xmi file, select the Rich XMI check box in the Environment Options dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Select the format for saving a project:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Packed MagicDraw File Format (<em>*.mdzip</em>) (default). <em>*.mdzip</em> is a MagicDraw native format. You should choose it if you intend to analyze the project's structure, xml, or for other purposes. You can open *.mdxml projects with other UML tools as well.</span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XML (<em>*.xml</em>). It is the same file format as <em>*.mdxml</em>, only that <em>*.mdxml</em> files can be opened with a modeling tool, whereas <em>*.xml</em> files can only be opened with an XML editor.</span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span><ac:image ac:title="Save dialog" ac:alt="Save dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b21069a7-3b65-449c-a02b-e39bedf3ebac"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the <strong>Create Backup File</strong> check box is selected in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, the modeling tool automatically creates a backup file of the previously saved project. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=286556268 space=MT version=16 -->
## PAGE 06159: Saving projects to an earlier version

- page_id: `286556268`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/286556268/Saving+projects+to+an+earlier+version
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:05:49.292+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects
- labels: ['msa', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can save both local and server projects to a previous major version, for example, from version 2026x to 2024x.

#### NOTE: Limitations

Limitations

- You can save projects only to the immediately preceding major version. For example, saving from version 2026x to 2022x is not supported.
- The functionality does not work with some modeling tool plugins. For example, saving UAF and BPMN2 projects to an older version is not supported.

To save a project to an earlier version

1. In the main menu, select File > Export To > Older Version . [ATTACHMENT filename='saving_projects_to_earlier_version.png']
2. In the open dialog, select the location where you want to save the project.
3. Click the Save button.

The project is saved in a new folder created in your selected location.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can save both local and server projects to a previous major version, for example, from version 2026x to 2024x.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e49f8b7-5fd0-4b37-857e-23175d693dbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Limitations</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f9ae79e3-6380-47db-9694-353049ee71f8"><p>You can save projects only to the immediately preceding major version. For example, saving from version 2026x to 2022x is not supported.</p></li><li data-uuid="4074f757-f939-4883-b7a5-c36680636c8e">The functionality does not work with some modeling tool plugins. For example, saving UAF and BPMN2 projects to an older version is not supported.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To save a project to an earlier version</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="96114dff-19e5-4425-88ff-c0c204cbaaf5">In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>Older Version</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="saving_projects_to_earlier_version.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="cfe3e3dc-a25c-4f50-bd25-f11d35825a29">In the open dialog, select the location where you want to save the project.</li><li data-uuid="d495882d-f98f-41a2-849f-6a189f38ba50">Click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The project is saved in a new folder created in your selected location.</p>
````

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To save the filter criteria

- On the Options toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] and then select Save Filter Criteria .

When exporting the table, only the filtered out columns and rows will be included in the result.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06161: Scaling images

- page_id: `243970817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970817/Scaling+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.550+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternative scaling methods

2050246330

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternative scaling methods

2050246342

2050246328

**On this page**

42

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternative scaling methods

2050246332

There are two scaling methods that you can use to scale an image using a given factor. For example, Method 1 provides height and width parameters (scaleWidth and scaleHeight), while Method 2 provides a single parameter that will scale both axes equally (scaleFactor). These three parameters must be positive real numbers.

###### **$image.scale(image, scaleWidth, scaleHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | scaleWidth | java.lang.Double | The width parameter of the image. |
|  | scaleHeight | java.lang.Double | The height parameter of the image. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.scale(image, scaleFactor)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | scaleFactor | java.lang.Double | The scaling parameter of the image. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.5)**, for example, to scale down the image to half the original size. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image scale function.

Use **$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.8, 0.6)**to scale the image’s width down to 80% and height to 60%. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image scale function.

#### NOTE: Alternative scaling methods

Alternative scaling methods

The images used in generated reports can also be scaled by specifying the **Exported Image Size** property in one of the following locations:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space='']**Properties** dialog , which is used to define image export properties for the Save as Image functionality.
- The Environment Option dialog. To open the dialog, in the main menu, select Options → Environment . Image export properties are located under the General group.

Note that the **Exported Image Size** setting is applied in generated reports only if you select **No resize** from the **Auto image size** drop down list located in the **Output Options** area of the **Report Wizard**.

#### Scaling Quality

Because the image tool provides several methods for scaling images, the quality of the scaled image depends on the algorithm that is used. To set the image scaling quality, $image.setScalingQuality() code must be inserted before scaling images. For example:

```text
$image.setScalingQuality(5)$image.scale(0.5)
```

The code above will set the image scaling quality to ‘highest’. The possible values are from 1 to 5, where 5 is ‘highest’ and 1 is ‘lowest’. The following table provides the description of each value.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | lowest' – Use one-step nearest neighbor interpolation. |
| 2 | 'low' – Use one-step bi-cubic interpolation. |
| 3 | 'medium – Use multi-step, bi-linear interpolation. |
| 4 | 'high' – Use area average image scaling algorithm. |
| 5 | 'highest' – Use a lossless transformation when the template is ODF, DOCX, or RTF; otherwise, area average image scaling algorithm will be used instead. |

Sample pictures from different image scaling qualities with their sizes scaled down to 50% are indicated in the pictures below (ranging from the value 1 ‘lowest’ to 5 ‘highest’):

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The following table provides the description of each value.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Value </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>1 </td><td>lowest' – Use one-step nearest <br />neighbor interpolation. </td></tr><tr><td>2 </td><td>'low' – Use one-step <br />bi-cubic interpolation.</td></tr><tr><td>3 </td><td>'medium – Use multi-step, <br />bi-linear interpolation. </td></tr><tr><td>4 </td><td>'high' – Use area average <br />image scaling algorithm. </td></tr><tr><td>5 </td><td>'highest' – Use a lossless transformation <br />when the template is ODF, DOCX, or RTF; <br />otherwise, area average image scaling <br />algorithm will be used instead.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Sample pictures from different image scaling qualities with their sizes scaled down to 50% are indicated in the pictures below (ranging from the value 1 ‘lowest’ to 5 ‘highest’):</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ScaleQuality1-3.png" /></ac:image><br />Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.</h6><p><br /><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ScaleQuality4-5.png" /></ac:image><br /><br />Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567571 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06162: Schema

- page_id: `249567571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567571/Schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:03.202+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Class with the stereotype XSDschema.

All schema global attributes and elements are mapped to the UML Attributes of this class.

The name of this class should match the file name or must be assigned to the component representing the file. The “xmlns” xml tags maps to a permission link with the stereotype «xmlns» and name, representing a given prefix.

The permission client is the schema class and supplier package with its name equal to the “xmlns” value.

#### PANEL: schema XML representation summary

schema XML representation summary

```text
<schema
```

```text
attributeFormDefault = (qualified | unqualified) : unqualified	blockDefault = (#all | List of (extension | restriction | substitution)) : ''
```

```text
elementFormDefault = (qualified | unqualified) : unqualified	finalDefault = (#all | List of (extension | restriction)) : ''
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
targetNamespace = anyURI
```

```text
version = token
```

```text
xml:lang = language
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: ((include | import | redefine | annotation)*, (((simpleType | complexType | group | attriuteGroup) | element | attribute | notation), annotation*)*)
```

```text
</schema>
```

#### PANEL: XML representation of the skeleton of a schema

XML representation of the skeleton of a schema

```text
<xs:schema
```

```text
xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"	targetNamespace="http://www.example.com/example">
```

```text
…
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### schema UML model example.

#### PANEL: schema XML code sample

schema XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com"xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"xmlns = "http://nomagic.com"attributeFormDefault = "qualified"blockDefault = "extension"
```

```text
elementFormDefault = "unqualified"finalDefault = "extension"
```

```text
targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com"version = "1.2"
```

```text
xml:lang = "EN" />
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566508 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06163: Script Task

- page_id: `249566508`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566508/Script+Task
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:05.971+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Activities > Tasks
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487024220

1487024221

1487024221

**Description**

A Script Task is executed by a business process engine. A modeler or an implementer defines a script in a language that the engine can interpret. When the task is ready to start, the engine will execute the script. When the script is completed, the task will also be completed.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487024219

**Related elements**

- Activities
- Task
- Service Task
- Send Task
- Receive Task
- User Task
- Manual Task
- Script Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using Tasks

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973755 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06164: Scripting languages performance

- page_id: `243973755`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973755/Scripting+languages+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:21.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When choosing a scripting language, we advise paying attention to its performance. For this, we have prepared performance measurements for built-in scripting languages.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The measurements are based on comparing the execution time of a script written in different languages and executed for 5000 elements. The example script is designed to retrieve a stereotype tag value and compare it to a string. All scripting languages execute the same logic using a different syntax for each scripting language. See the script example written in Groovy language below:

```groovy
0 && value[0] == "tagValue"]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">When choosing a scripting language, we advise paying attention to its performance. For this, we have prepared performance measurements for built-in scripting languages.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Scripting_languages_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The measurements are based on comparing the execution time of a script written in different languages and executed for 5000 elements. The example script is designed to retrieve a stereotype tag value and compare it to a string. All scripting languages execute the same logic using a different syntax for each scripting language. See the script example written in Groovy language below: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a13b45f-048d-4ec4-bf74-dd7a8a92ae0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">groovy</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Groovy</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//retrieve a value from a stereotype tag and compare it with the string "tagValue" 
def value = com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper.getStereotypePropertyValue(element, "stereotypeName", "propertyTagName") 
value.size() > 0 && value[0] == "tagValue"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965764 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06165: Search Results tab

- page_id: `243965764`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965764/Search+Results+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:44.956+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

635526173

635526175

635526174

INLINE

The Search Results tab is used to display search results. This tab opens only after [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space=''] by pressing Ctrl+Shift+F, or by clicking **Edit** > **Find**.

The tab is not displayed when performing a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick Find dialog' space='']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Search Results** tab consists of two components:

- 
- 

##### #searchresulttoolbarSearch Results tab toolbar

The Search Results tab toolbars contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Find... | Opens the Find dialog. |
|  | Clear Results | Clears the results of the previous search. |
|  | Rerun Search | Reruns a search with the already specified search criteria. Search results are updated according to changes done in the model. |
|  | Save Search Results | Saves as a smart package either the search options or the search results. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.Show Full Types - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree. |

The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Search Results tree. For more information about commands available from the shortcut menu in the Model Browser, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements in the Model Browser' space=''].

##### #searchresulttreeWorking with model elements in the Search Results tree

The search results in the **Search Results** tree are displayed in the following two packages:

- Found in loaded diagrams – symbols that have been found in the diagram(s) are displayed in this package.
- Found in model – elements that have been found in the model data are displayed in this package.

635526171

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966044 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06166: Searching

- page_id: `243966044`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966044/Searching
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:51.978+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

468780347

468780350

468780349

INLINE

Searching the modeling tool is designed to search within model elements, as well as element properties. You can also perform a quick search without defining any advanced options, or you can replace any text quickly using the **Find and Replace** type of search.

You can read more about types of search dialogs in the related pages.

The search is performed as a background task; while searching, you may still read the model and browse it. If you make changes in the model, the search stops and presents incomplete search results in the **Search Results** tab of the **Model Browser**. You need to restart the search to get the full list of results.

If there is a large number of search results to display (more than 1000), the following message appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Click **Yes** to continue searching, or click **No** to stop searching and correct the query.

If the indexing of model elements is in progress, the following **Question** dialog appears before performing a search in the **Find** and**Find and Replace** dialogs:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click**Yes** to perform a search while indexing is still in progress, or click**No** to stop searching and wait for the indexing operation to complete in order to prevent slow searching.

468780346

**Related pages**

You need to restart the search to get the full list of results.</p><p>If there is a large number of search results to display (more than 1000), the following message appears:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Many results found message" ac:alt="Many results found message"><ri:attachment ri:filename="many_results_found_message.png" /></ac:image> Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Yes</strong> to continue searching, or click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">No</strong> to stop searching and correct the query.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>If the indexing of model elements is in progress, the following <strong>Question</strong> dialog appears before performing a search in the <strong>Find</strong> and<strong> Find and Replace</strong> dialogs:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog_when_searching.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">Click<strong> Yes</strong> to perform a search while indexing is still in progress, or click<strong> No</strong> to stop searching and wait for the indexing operation to complete in order to prevent slow searching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34f2d1df-33c6-47f6-a220-dded2080479b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">468780346</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5917640c-094e-4012-864f-2b7f7b4e8686" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966089 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06167: Searching in diagrams

- page_id: `243966089`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966089/Searching+in+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:52.884+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Searching tips

586610874

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Searching tips

586610876

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Searching tips

586610875

The **Find in Diagram** command enables you to search for textual information in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use this command to find a specific text phrase in a diagram and navigate through the highlighted search results.

To find text in a diagram

1. Open the diagram in which you want to perform the search.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='find_in_diagram_button.png'] in the diagram toolbar.
  - On the main menu, click Edit > Find in Diagram .
  - Press Ctrl+F.
3. In the search box , type the text you want to find.
4. Use [ATTACHMENT filename='find_previous.png'] (Shift+F3) / [ATTACHMENT filename='find_next.png'] (F3) to navigate through highlighted search results.

#### TIP: Searching tips

Searching tips

- You can search in several open diagrams at once. Typing a search phrase in a subsequent diagram does not undo the search results in a previous diagram.
- When you press Ctrl+F with multiple open diagrams, the search starts in an active diagram.
- To search for textual information in the whole model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button.png'] in the diagram search bar and specify the search criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space=''] .

After typing a search phrase, the search results are highlighted in yellow and the diagram or table scrolls to the first search result (highlighted in orange), as shown below. Diagram symbols or table rows that do not contain your search phrase are shaded. A diagram symbol is also shaded if the search phrase is found in the symbol compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### How to use the Find feature in diagrams and navigate the search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
