````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3787c8e2-ff1c-4b0c-bb3e-7957de168ea7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610874</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1660885-fdde-4a18-a029-4279e16b1883"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610876</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="415c1c49-17d3-48be-aad1-3b0a6b9b8f19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610875</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Find in Diagram</strong> command enables you to search for textual <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99b2bb6f-38e8-4803-9346-c998b2e3d20a">information</ac:inline-comment-marker> in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use this command to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="82535d25-0915-44c2-a949-d768ef89f9b5">find a specific text phrase in a diagram and navigate through the highlighted search results</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find text in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the diagram in which you want to perform the search.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_in_diagram_button.png" /></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find in Diagram</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press Ctrl+F.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the search <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90d72029-0ec3-4204-aaea-173db7073a9c">box</ac:inline-comment-marker>, type the text you want to find.</li><li>Use <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="10"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_previous.png" /></ac:image> (Shift+F3)<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d9fcc5ec-31a1-479a-9e73-c05e5f298877">/</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_next.png" /></ac:image> (F3) to navigate through highlighted <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edbb29db-4c97-477f-a78f-a56fa6749c2d">search results.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edbb29db-4c97-477f-a78f-a56fa6749c2d"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93f97664-c6e3-4537-a178-61dd205ddcfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Searching tips</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can search in several open diagrams at once. Typing a search phrase in a subsequent diagram does not undo the search results in a previous diagram.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="327fddd8-d31d-4385-9099-6e7c1a4d64c7">When you </ac:inline-comment-marker>press Ctrl+F with multiple open diagrams, the search starts in an active diagram.</li><li>To search for textual information in the whole model, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button.png" /></ac:image> in the diagram search bar and specify the search criteria in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>After typing a search phrase, the search results are highlighted in yellow and the diagram or table scrolls to the first search result (highlighted in orange), as shown below. Diagram symbols or table rows that do not contain your search phrase are shaded. A diagram symbol is also shaded if the search phrase is found in the symbol compartment.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_in_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">How to use the Find feature in diagrams and navigate the search results.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

918471926

918471928

918471927

The Security (Sc) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges between resources and Operational Performers. It illustrates the security assets, security constraints, security controls, families, and measures required to address specific security concerns.

918471925

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564537 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06169: Security Connectivity

- page_id: `249564537`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564537/Security+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:18.677+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918511719

918511721

918511720

**Description**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges across resources and across performers. It lists security exchanges across security assets; the applicable security controls; and the security enclaves that house the producers and consumers of the exchanges. Measurements can optionally be included.

**Implementation**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity (Resource) Table' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table' space=''] .
- Reports (.xlsx): - Security Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Security Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

It lists security exchanges across security assets; the applicable security controls; and the security enclaves that house the producers and consumers of the exchanges. Measurements can optionally be included.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Connectivity (Resource) Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Connectivity Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Role-based Connectivity Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Reports (.xlsx):<br />- Security Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.<br />- Security Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_connectivity.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Security Connectivity <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06170: Security Constraints

- page_id: `249564567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564567/Security+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:19.860+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918678581

918678584

918678582

**Definition**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain shows the security-related policy, guidance, laws and regulations as applicable to resources. It Specifies textual rules/non-functional requirements that are security constraints on resources, information and data (e.g. security-related in the form of rules (e.g. access control policy). A common way of representing access control policy is through the use of XACML (eXtensible Access Control Markup Language), it is expected that implementations of UAF allow users to link security constraints to external files represented in XACML.

**Implementation**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints table' space=''] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Constraints diagram

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

918678580

**Description**

The Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of security elements.

The intended usage of the Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

918798584

918798586

918798585

**Description**

The **Security Processes (Sc-Pr)**domain shows the specification of the Security Control families, security controls, and measures required to address a specific security baseline. It provides a set of Security Controls and any possible enhancements as applicable to assets. The activity diagram describes operational or resource level processes that apply (operational level) or implement (resource level) security controls/enhancements to assets located in enclaves and across enclaves. This Security Process domain can be instantiated either as a variant of an activity/flow diagram or as a hierarchical work breakdown structure.

**Implementation**

The Security Processes (Sc-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram' space=''] .

918798583

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564602 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06174: Security Structure

- page_id: `249564602`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564602/Security+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:20.728+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918973399

918973401

918973400

**Description**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain shows the structure of security information and where it is used at the operational and resource level. It captures the allocation of assets (operational and resource, information, and data) across the security enclaves, shows applicable security controls necessary to protect organizations, systems, and information during processing, while in storage (bdd), and during transmission (flows on an ibd). This view also captures Asset Aggregation and allocates the usage of the aggregated information at a location.

**Implementation**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Structure diagram

## PAGE 06175: Security Taxonomy

- page_id: `249564620`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564620/Security+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:21.013+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919067284

919067286

919067285

**Description**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain shows the security assets and security enclaves. It defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners that are available to implement security, security constraints (policy, guidance, laws and regulations) and details where they are located (security enclaves).

**Implementation**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy table' space=''] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy table

565418804

565418806

565418805

**Description**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain depicts the mapping of a risk to each of the following: risk owner, risk mitigations, and affected asset roles.

**Implementation**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix' space=''] shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix' space=''] shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970650 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06177: Select Element Scope pane

- page_id: `243970650`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970650/Select+Element+Scope+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:52.021+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Select Element Scope** pane (see the figure below) allows you to select the scope of the modeling tool's data to generate a report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element Scope pane.

The table below describes the detail of each component in the **Select Element Scope** pane.

| Component | Name description |
| --- | --- |
| Select Element Scope pane | A group for selecting an element scope. |
| Search for scope | Select Package for listing only Package elements in the tree. Select Any Element for listing any element in the tree. |
| Apply Filter option | Select to apply the filter to the tree. |
| Filter button | Select the filter options: Hide Uncommon Elements and Exclude Auxiliary Resources. |
|  | Add the selected element in the tree. |
|  | Recursively add the selected elements in the tree. |
|  | Remove the selected scope element from the list. |
|  | Remove all scope elements from the list. |
| Selected elements pane | List added element(s) in the tree. |
| Selected Scope Options | Generate Recursively - Recursively generate elements from the selected scope elements.Include Auxiliary Resources - Include auxiliary resources found in the selected scope elements. |

You can perform the following operations in the **Select Element Scope** pane:

- Add packages into the Selected elements list.
- Remove packages from the Selected elements list.
- Select or clear the Generate Recursively option.
- Include auxiliary resources.
- Show and search for package elements.

To add packages

- 
  - In the Select Element Scope pane (see the figure above), select the packages from the tree and click Add [ATTACHMENT filename='Add Button.png'] or Add Recursively [ATTACHMENT filename='Add Recursive Button.png'] to add them into the Selected elements list.

To remove packages

- 
  - In the Select Element Scope pane (see figure above), select the packages from the tree and click Remove [ATTACHMENT filename='Remove Selected Scope Button.png'] or Remove All [ATTACHMENT filename='Remove All Scopes Button.png'] to remove them from the Selected elements list.

To select or clear the **Generate Recursively** option

- 
  - Select or clear the Generate Recursively check box (see the figure above).

To include auxiliary resources

- 
  - Select the Include Auxiliary Resources check box and add auxiliary resources into the Selected elements list (see the figure above).

To show and search for package elements

- 
  - In the Search for scope, select the Package option and enter the search keyword in the search field (see the figure above).

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The figure above shows the **UML 2 Elements** package and the **Generate Recursively** option are selected. It means that the **UML 2 Elements** package and its subpackages will be generated in the report.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane (see the figure below) allows you to select the scope of the modeling tool's data to generate a report.</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Element Scope Pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 60.0px;">The Select Element Scope pane.</h6><p>The table below describes the detail of each component in the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 98.563%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 22.4893%;" /><col style="width: 77.5107%;" /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Component </th><th>Name description </th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane<strong><br /></strong></td><td colspan="1">A group for selecting an element scope.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Search for </strong>scope</td><td colspan="1">Select <strong>Package</strong> for listing only Package elements in the tree. Select <strong>Any Element</strong> for listing any element in the tree.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Apply Filter</strong> option</td><td colspan="1">Select to apply the filter to the tree.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">Filter button</td><td colspan="1">Select the filter options: <strong>Hide Uncommon Elements</strong> and <strong>Exclude Auxiliary Resources</strong>.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add Button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Add the selected element in the tree.</p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add Recursive Button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Recursively add the selected elements in the tree.</p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Remove Selected Scope Button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remove the selected scope element from the list.</p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Remove All Scopes Button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Remove all scope elements from the list.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Selected elements</strong> pane</p></div></td><td colspan="1">List added element(s) in the tree.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Selected Scope Options</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Generate Recursively - Recursively generate elements from the selected scope elements.</li><li>Include Auxiliary Resources - Include auxiliary resources found in the selected scope elements.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>You can perform the following operations in the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane:</p><ul><li>Add packages into the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li><li>Remove packages from the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li><li>Select or clear the <strong>Generate Recursively</strong> option.</li><li>Include auxiliary resources.</li><li>Show and search for package elements.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add packages</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>In the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane (see the figure above), select the packages from the tree and click <strong>Add </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add Button.png" /></ac:image> or<strong> Add Recursively</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add Recursive Button.png" /></ac:image> to add them into the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove packages</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>In the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> pane (see figure above), select the packages from the tree and click <strong>Remove </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Remove Selected Scope Button.png" /></ac:image> or<strong> Remove All </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Remove All Scopes Button.png" /></ac:image> to remove them from the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To select or clear the <strong>Generate Recursively</strong> option</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Select or clear the <strong>Generate Recursively</strong> check box (see the figure above).</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To include auxiliary resources</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Select the <strong>Include Auxiliary Resources</strong> check box and add auxiliary resources into the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list (see the figure above).</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show and search for package elements</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>In the<strong> Search for </strong>scope, select the <strong>Package</strong> option and enter the search keyword in the search field (see the figure above).<br /> </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2a23316-67f9-4ceb-85ce-895af4ae87be"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The figure above shows the <strong>UML 2 Elements</strong> package and the <strong>Generate Recursively</strong> option are selected. It means that the <strong>UML 2 Elements</strong> package and its subpackages will be generated in the report.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565009 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06178: Select Framework Views dialog

- page_id: `249565009`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565009/Select+Framework+Views+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:22:09.787+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the **Select Framework Views** dialog.

The **Select Framework Views**dialog is displayed when the **Select Framework Views** option in the **New Project** dialog is set to *True*. By default this option is set to *True*. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to *False*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog

In the **Select Framework Views**dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The dialog for selecting views for your new project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog is displayed when the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> option in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog is set to <em>True</em>. By default this option is set to <em>True</em>. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to <em>False</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog</h6><p>In the <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_framework_views.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The dialog for selecting views for your new project</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965819 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06179: Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `243965819`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965819/Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:29:12.841+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

false

msa,mcse

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Full FeaturedQuick StartSoftware ArchitectSystem Analyst | List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration. |
| Expert | Choose Expert mode only if you are an advanced user. All features and commands will be loaded. If you are a new user, you should not select Expert mode. The non-expert mode only loads the basic and most important commands and features. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, show, or hide selected commands. |

false

msosa

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Business Analyst | This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| Business Architect | This perspective provides features for Business Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| DoDAF 2.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF 2.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF 2.0-related features are hidden. |
| DODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF-related features are hidden. |
| MODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to MODAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-MODAF-related features are hidden. |
| NAF 4.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF 4.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF 4.0-related features are hidden. |
| NAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden. |
| SOA Engineer | This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. This is the default Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Cameo Enterprise Architect configuration. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| Software Architect | This perspective is dedicated to Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer roles that design and implement the project. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| System Analyst | This perspective is a set of roles primarily dedicated to eliciting requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| System Engineer | This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. SysML support is accessible. Requirements elicitation and modeling features are highlighted. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-system-modeling-related features are hidden. |
| TOGAF Architect | This perspective provides features for TOGAF Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects with all the UAF products available. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. |
| EA-BPMN Architect* | This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. |
| Zachman Framework | This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands. |

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565013 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06180: Select Symbol dialog

- page_id: `249565013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565013/Select+Symbol+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:31.389+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['select-symbol-dialog', 'affiliation', 'frame', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1015178682

1015178684

1015178683

Use **Select Symbol** dialog to apply military symbols for resources.

To open the **Select Symbol** dialog, do either

- Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
- Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
- Open the Specification window of any UAF element. Select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] . The **Select Symbol** dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Select Symbol** dialog consists of these four components:

##### Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree

Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.

##### Quick filter

You can search for symbols in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs by using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy. For example, type "1.X.3.2.2.1.3".
- Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols. For example, type "*Frying" if you want to find "Fixed Wing" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Affiliation area

Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.

##### Frame area

Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.

##### Preview area

You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.

1015178681

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5246dee0-c92f-4786-b75e-5ed56e4d6c94"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1015178682</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8a550b4-fdab-42ee-8c4e-3b8bb4a39d9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1015178684</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73ae2b22-ad08-4c69-94fe-f3c375d65607"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1015178683</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use <strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog to apply military symbols for resources.</p><p>To open the <strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.</li><li>Select resource and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png" /></ac:image> on the smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>Open the Specification window of any UAF element. Select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png" /></ac:image>.<br />The <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog</span> opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Select Symbol dialog" ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog consists of these four components:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f3a7da5-9780-4043-9b1d-1479391dbc99" /></p><h3>Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree</h3><p>Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.</p><h3>Quick filter</h3><p>You can search for symbols in the <strong>Whole hierarchy</strong> or <strong>Recently used</strong> tabs by using one of the following way:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy. </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35bee693-e8a4-4b4e-8c4f-2bcc8ed0b2c3"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;1.X.3.2.2.1.3&quot;.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660312ee-045d-4875-a612-1aa2773946ac"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;*Frying&quot; if you want to find &quot;Fixed Wing&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Affiliation area</span><br /></span></h3><p>Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.</p><h3>Frame area</h3><p>Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.</p><h3>Preview area</h3><p>You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="713b5189-b80f-4605-8096-c517eb513212"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1015178681</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying military symbols" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973759 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06181: Selecting a default macro language

- page_id: `243973759`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973759/Selecting+a+default+macro+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:21.211+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the**Environment Options** dialog to select a default macro language.

To select a default macro language

1. Click Options > Environment on the main menu. The Environment Options dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='tools-options.png']
2. Select the Macros node. [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-env-options.png']
3. Click the box next to the Default Macro Language box to see a list of supported programming languages. [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-env-options-b.png']
4. Select the preferred language.
5. Click OK to save the selected language as the default macro language.

- Groovy is the default macro language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language.
- Macro Engine currently supports BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, and Jython only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog to select a default macro language.</p><p><br /></p><p>To select a default macro language</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> on the main menu. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Environment Options Menu" ac:alt="Environment Options Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools-options.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the <strong>Macros</strong> node.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Environment Options Dialog" ac:alt="The Environment Options Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-env-options.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the box next to the <strong>Default Macro Language</strong> box to see a list of supported programming languages.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Selecting Macro Language" ac:alt="Selecting Macro Language"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-env-options-b.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the preferred language.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to save the selected language as the default macro language.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eaf72942-6f5c-4c81-ad0f-fd5db9e7e781"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Groovy is the default macro language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language.</li><li>Macro Engine currently supports BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, and Jython only.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561323 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06182: Selecting actual Connectors in the Containment tree

- page_id: `249561323`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561323/Selecting+actual+Connectors+in+the+Containment+tree
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:15:10.742+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram > Implied Connectors
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to see an actual Connectors underneath the implied relation without displaying them, you can select them in the Containment tree. How to select actual Connector in Containment tree read the following procedure.

To select actual Connector in the Containment tree

1. Select the implied relation.
2. From its shortcut menu, point to the Underlying Connections command group, and select an appropriate Connector. Actual Connector is selected in the Containment tree.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Underlying Connectors command group provides a list of actual Connectors which are underneath the implied relation.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property path notation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967000 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06184: Selecting columns to display

- page_id: `243967000`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967000/Selecting+columns+to+display
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:16.917+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, the Instance Table shows nine columns (including # (row number), Name (instance name), and the columns for instance slot values) that correspond to the first seven Data type attributes of the specified classifier. The rest of the columns are by default hidden, though you can show them if there is a need.

You can show any column that corresponds to one of the following:

- .
- 
- 
- 

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

display_attributesTo display attributes of the classifier

1. On the table toolbar, click the Columns button and then click to select the check boxes beside the attributes you want to show. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_columns.png']

display_properties_tagsTo display properties and tags

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Select Columns** >**Metaproperties** tab. In the **Select Columns** dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the Instance Table by selecting the appropriate check boxes of the properties and tags. All columns that you have chosen to display also appears on the menu of the **Columns** button.

nested_propertiesTo display nested properties of the classifier

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Select Columns** > **Properties** tab. In the **Select Columns** dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the Instance Table by selecting the appropriate check boxes of the nested properties. To specify nested columns, click the **Create with Parts** button, when . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you add a new attribute to the classifier and need to display the corresponding column in the Instance Table, open the**Select Columns** dialog and click to select the appropriate check box.

Keep in mind that columns corresponding to newly created attributes at first are available only in the **Select Columns** dialog (until you show them).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966480 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06185: Selecting criteria

- page_id: `243966480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966480/Selecting+criteria
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:32.693+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308994112

308994115

308994111

**On this page**

308994113

To create a Dependency Matrix, first of all, you need to define which data you wish to display on its pane. Select row and column element types, row and column scope, the direction of the relationships as well as the dependency criteria for this. Be advised that the easiest way to define all the above-mentioned criteria, except the type of the relationship direction, is using the drag-and-drop operation.

A lot of properties of the Dependency Matrix can be specified in thematrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

You can open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] in one of the following ways:

- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix shortcut menu' space='']
- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space='']
- Press Enter in the active matrix.

##### Specifying element type

To specify a row/column element type

Do either:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select one or more elements which types you wish to see on your matrix and drag them to the Row Element Type / Column Element Type box in the Criteria area.

- For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.
- If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the **Row Element Type**/**Column Element Type** box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Row Element Type**/**Column Element Type** box and in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='']**Select Element Type** dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Select the**Include subtypes**check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.Select the**Include Custom Types**check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.

- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , click the Row Element Type / Column Element Type property value cell, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button, and in the opened dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click Close .

##### Dependency Criteria dialogSpecifying dependency criterion

To specify which relationships you want to display in the matrix

1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button next to Dependency Criteria .
2. Select the relationships you want to display. [ATTACHMENT filename='dependency_criteria_selecting_relationships.png']
3. In the Properties column, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button to select the properties of the relationship. When you're done, click Close . [ATTACHMENT filename='relationship_properties_updated.png']
  - Include Relationship Subtypes - select to include the subtypes of the selected relationship types.
  - Include Relationship Custom Types - select to include the custom relationship types that extend the selected relationship types.
  - Filter by Property Value - select properties to use as additional relationship filters.
  - **Include Aggregation and Composition** - select to include Aggregation and Composition relationships along with the Association relationship. The **Include Aggregation and Composition** option can be specified in the **Relationship Properties** dialog only if the Association or custom Association relationship is selected as a Relation Criterion.
4. In the Style column, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying relationship line styles' space=''] .
5. When you're done, click Close , then OK .

##### Specifying relationship direction

To specify a relationship direction

Do either:

- Select a direction from the Direction drop-down list in the Criteria area. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_criteria_direction.png']
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , click the Direction property value cell and select what direction relationships you wish to see on your matrix. Click Close .

##### Specifying what elements are visible in the matrix

To specify what elements you wish to see in the matrix: related, non-related, or all

Do either:

- From the Show Elements drop-down list in the Criteria area, select
  - With relations if you need to see only related elements from the selected scope.
  - Without relations if you need to see only non-related elements from the selected scope.
  - All if you need to see both related and non-related elements from the selected scope.
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], click the **Show Elements** property value cell and choose one of the values described above. Click **Close**.

After the **Show Elements** value is changed, you should always refresh a matrix.

Once criteria are specified, you can create the matrix. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When a Dependency Matrix contains a large number of rows and/or columns, it may become difficult to work with, as you need to scroll in order to see certain parts of it. In this case, you can select to exclude cells (either rows or columns) that contain relationships and make your Dependency Matrix view more compact.

To specify what cells to display on the matrix

1. From the Show Elements drop-down list in the Criteria area, select either [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Columns without relations** – to show columns without relationships and all rows.
  - **Rows without relations** – to show rows without relationships and all columns.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to refresh the matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47a3e539-bb37-4951-abb0-b48d6694efd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994112</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e182127-85b7-47c6-b05c-a1548d961ee6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994115</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="086143f4-3bdf-4d55-ab33-3a7b40c718c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994111</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa5fc39f-c0bb-4855-a8ee-8b8028b42db7" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="750d4234-4f16-4e2b-bdf9-e4a50047d588"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994113</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a Dependency Matrix, first of all, you need to define which data you wish to display on its pane. Select row and column element types, row and column scope, the direction of the relationships as well as the dependency criteria for this. Be advised that the easiest way to define all the above-mentioned criteria, except the type of the relationship direction, is using the drag-and-drop operation.</p><p>A lot of properties of the Dependency Matrix can be specified in the<span> matrix </span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02c6a05d-c8a9-49f0-83a9-4d4c7807ea63"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>From the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix shortcut menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[matrix shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>From the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[matrix toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b2d5989a-d917-4954-a126-220fe9719243">Press Enter in the active matrix.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Specifying element type</h3><p>To specify a row/column element type</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, select one or more elements which types you wish to see on your matrix and drag them to the <strong>Row Element Type</strong>/<strong>Column Element Type</strong> box in the <strong>Criteria</strong> area.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="851f1af8-ced8-4532-8dc3-e2ad2cd88ae2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bb3358fd-a9f6-4656-9d55-132dabcb5d8d">If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the <strong>Row Element Type</strong>/<strong>Column Element Type</strong> box.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_criteria_row_element_type.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li><p>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Row Element Type</strong>/<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Column Element Type</strong> box and in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_types_dialog_new.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb05b02-bf43-4df6-8bcc-bcb5fb812b3d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Include subtypes</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> check box t<span>o display the subtypes of the selected element types.</span></span></li></ul><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Include Custom Types</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> check box t<span>o display the custom types extending the selected element types.</span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li>Open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Row Element Type</strong>/<strong>Column Element Type</strong> property value cell, then click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button, and in the opened dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ul><h3><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64790c61-0d3f-4fbc-94b5-e73ef43adb6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Dependency Criteria dialog</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specifying dependency criterion</h3><p><br /></p><p>To specify which relationships you want to display in the matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to <strong>Dependency Criteria</strong>.</li><li>Select the relationships you want to display.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency_criteria_selecting_relationships.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Properties </strong> column, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to select the properties of the relationship. When you're done, click <strong> Close</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relationship_properties_updated.png" /></ac:image><ul><li><strong>Include Relationship Subtypes</strong> - select to include the subtypes of the selected relationship types.</li><li><strong>Include Relationship Custom Types</strong> - select to include the custom relationship types that extend the selected relationship types. </li><li><strong>Filter by Property Value</strong> - select properties to use as additional relationship filters. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Include Aggregation and Composition</strong> - select to include Aggregation and Composition relationships along with the Association relationship. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34182946-c37e-498c-b789-051d770b1e8e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Include Aggregation and Composition</strong> option can be specified in the <strong>Relationship Properties</strong> dialog only if the Association or custom Association relationship is selected as a Relation Criterion.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Style</strong> column, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying relationship line styles" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[select how the relationship is represented in the matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>.<span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> </span></li><li>When you're done, click <strong>Close</strong>, then <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Specifying relationship direction</h3><p>To specify a relationship direction</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li>Select a direction from the <strong>Direction</strong> drop-down list in the <strong>Criteria</strong> area.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_criteria_direction.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Direction</strong> property value cell and select what direction relationships you wish to see on your matrix. Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ul><h3><br class="_mce_tagged_br" />Specifying what elements are visible in the matrix</h3><p>To specify what elements you wish to see in the matrix: related, non-related, or all</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li>From the <strong>Show Elements</strong> drop-down list in the <strong>Criteria</strong> area, select<ul><li><strong>With relations</strong> if you need to see only related elements from the selected scope.</li><li><strong>Without relations</strong> if you need to see only non-related elements from the selected scope. </li><li><strong>All</strong> if you need to see both related and non-related elements from the selected scope.</li></ul></li><li><p>Open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Show Elements</strong> property value cell and choose one of the values described above. Click <strong>Close</strong>.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cc5ce69-693a-48d7-8236-9d917585f5e6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After the <strong>Show Elements</strong> value is changed, you should always refresh a matrix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Once criteria are specified, you can create the matrix. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix toolbar" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Notification to refresh dependency matrix after changing dependency criteria" ac:alt="Notification to refresh dependency matrix after changing dependency criteria"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dep_matrix_refresh.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> <br /> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> <br /> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When a Dependency Matrix contains a large number of rows and/or columns, it may become difficult to work with, as you need to scroll in order to see certain parts of it. In this case, you can select to exclude cells (either rows or columns) that contain relationships and make your Dependency Matrix view more compact. <br /> </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To specify what cells to display on the matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li class="confluence-link">From the <strong>Show Elements</strong> drop-down list in the <strong>Criteria</strong> area, select either<br /> <br /><ul><li class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>Columns without relations</strong> </span> – to show columns without relationships and all rows.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>Rows without relations</strong> – to show rows without relationships and all columns.<br /> <br /> </span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting to Show Cells Without Relationships" ac:alt="Selecting to Show Cells Without Relationships" ac:height="107" ac:width="235"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_cells_without_relations.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to refresh the matrix.</span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968674 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06186: Selecting destination model

- page_id: `243968674`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968674/Selecting+destination+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.814+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the third step of the wizard, you can choose the package that will contain the generated model. Additionally, you can specify the following options:

Select the **Place transformation model in package** option button to specify the package into which the source will be transformed. Select an existing package or create a new one.

Select the **Transform in place** option, if you want the source model to be edited.

- If you choose the in-place transformation, the model part selected as the transformation source, will be edited directly, and you will not retain your original model. So, please, be careful with this option.

- If you choose the destination package, the source model will be copied to it and the transformation will be performed on this copy. Hence you will retain your source model and get a resulting model and traces will be established between elements in these model parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following operations are available in the**Select source**and**Select destination**steps:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select type mappings). |
| Finish | Finish the transformation configuration. All other options will be set by default.The Model Transformations Wizard exits and transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the third step of the wizard, you can choose the package that will contain the generated model. Additionally, you can specify the following options:</p><p>Select the <strong>Place transformation model in package</strong> option button to specify the package into which the source will be transformed. Select an existing package or create a new one.</p><p>Select the <strong>Transform in place</strong> option, if you want the source model to be edited.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca4b2c3b-3966-408e-96e8-bfd1676a8374"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you choose the in-place transformation, the model part selected as the transformation source, will be edited directly, and you will not retain your original model. So, please, be careful with this option.</li></ul><ul><li>If you choose the destination package, the source model will be copied to it and the transformation will be performed on this copy. Hence you will retain your source model and get a resulting model and traces will be established between elements in these model parts.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_3rd_step.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">The following operations are available in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">Select source </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">and</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a"> Select destination</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a"> steps:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td>Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select type mappings).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the transformation configuration. All other options will be set by default.</p><p>The Model Transformations Wizard exits and transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
